translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A roll of the birth of Jesus Christ, son of David, son of Abraham.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Abraham begat Isaac, and Isaac begat Jacob, and Jacob begat Judah and his brethren,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Judah begat Pharez and Zarah of Tamar, and Pharez begat Hezron, and Hezron begat Ram,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Ram begat Amminadab, and Amminadab begat Nahshon, and Nahshon begat Salmon,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Salmon begat Boaz of Rahab, and Boaz begat Obed of Ruth, and Obed begat Jesse,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jesse begat David the king. And David the king begat Solomon, of her [who had been] Uriah's,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Solomon begat Rehoboam, and Rehoboam begat Abijah, and Abijah begat Asa,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Asa begat Jehoshaphat, and Jehoshaphat begat Joram, and Joram begat Uzziah,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Uzziah begat Jotham, and Jotham begat Ahaz, and Ahaz begat Hezekiah,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Hezekiah begat Manasseh, and Manasseh begat Amon, and Amon begat Josiah,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Josiah begat Jeconiah and his brethren, at the Babylonian removal.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And after the Babylonian removal, Jeconiah begat Shealtiel, and Shealtiel begat Zerubbabel,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Zerubbabel begat Abiud, and Abiud begat Eliakim, and Eliakim begat Azor,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Azor begat Sadok, and Sadok begat Achim, and Achim begat Eliud,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Eliud begat Eleazar, and Eleazar begat Matthan, and Matthan begat Jacob,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jacob begat Joseph, the husband of Mary, of whom was begotten Jesus, who is named Christ.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: All the generations, therefore, from Abraham unto David [are] fourteen generations, and from David unto the Babylonian removal fourteen generations, and from the Babylonian removal unto the Christ, fourteen generations.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And of Jesus Christ, the birth was thus: For his mother Mary having been betrothed to Joseph, before their coming together she was found to have conceived from the Holy Spirit,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Joseph her husband being righteous, and not willing to make her an example, did wish privately to send her away.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And on his thinking of these things, lo, a messenger of the Lord in a dream appeared to him, saying, 'Joseph, son of David, thou mayest not fear to receive Mary thy wife, for that which in her was begotten [is] of the Holy Spirit,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and she shall bring forth a son, and thou shalt call his name Jesus, for he shall save his people from their sins.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And all this hath come to pass, that it may be fulfilled that was spoken by the Lord through the prophet, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Lo, the virgin shall conceive, and she shall bring forth a son, and they shall call his name Emmanuel,' which is, being interpreted 'With us [he is] God.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Joseph, having risen from the sleep, did as the messenger of the Lord directed him, and received his wife,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and did not know her till she brought forth her son -- the first-born, and he called his name Jesus.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus having been born in Beth-Lehem of Judea, in the days of Herod the king, lo, mages from the east came to Jerusalem,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: saying, 'Where is he who was born king of the Jews? for we saw his star in the east, and we came to bow to him.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Herod the king having heard, was stirred, and all Jerusalem with him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having gathered all the chief priests and scribes of the people, he was inquiring from them where the Christ is born.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they said to him, 'In Beth-Lehem of Judea, for thus it hath been written through the prophet,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And thou, Beth-Lehem, the land of Judah, thou art by no means the least among the leaders of Judah, for out of thee shall come one leading, who shall feed My people Israel.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then Herod, privately having called the mages, did inquire exactly from them the time of the appearing star,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having sent them to Beth-Lehem, he said, 'Having gone -- inquire ye exactly for the child, and whenever ye may have found, bring me back word, that I also having come may bow to him.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they, having heard the king, departed, and lo, the star, that they did see in the east, did go before them, till, having come, it stood over where the child was.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having seen the star, they rejoiced with exceeding great joy,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having come to the house, they found the child with Mary his mother, and having fallen down they bowed to him, and having opened their treasures, they presented to him gifts, gold, and frankincense, and myrrh,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having been divinely warned in a dream not to turn back unto Herod, through another way they withdrew to their own region.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And on their having withdrawn, lo, a messenger of the Lord doth appear in a dream to Joseph, saying, 'Having risen, take the child and his mother, and flee to Egypt, and be thou there till I may speak to thee, for Herod is about to seek the child to destroy him.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he, having risen, took the child and his mother by night, and withdrew to Egypt,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he was there till the death of Herod, that it might be fulfilled that was spoken by the Lord through the prophet, saying, 'Out of Egypt I did call My Son.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then Herod, having seen that he was deceived by the mages, was very wroth, and having sent forth, he slew all the male children in Beth-Lehem, and in all its borders, from two years and under, according to the time that he inquired exactly from the mages.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then was fulfilled that which was spoken by Jeremiah the prophet, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'A voice in Ramah was heard -- lamentation and weeping and much mourning -- Rachel weeping [for] her children, and she would not be comforted because they are not.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Herod having died, lo, a messenger of the Lord in a dream doth appear to Joseph in Egypt,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: saying, 'Having risen, take the child and his mother, and be going to the land of Israel, for they have died -- those seeking the life of the child.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he, having risen, took the child and his mother, and came to the land of Israel,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having heard that Archelaus doth reign over Judea instead of Herod his father, he was afraid to go thither, and having been divinely warned in a dream, he withdrew to the parts of Galilee,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and coming, he dwelt in a city named Nazareth, that it might be fulfilled that was spoken through the prophets, that 'A Nazarene he shall be called.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And in those days cometh John the Baptist, proclaiming in the wilderness of Judea,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and saying, 'Reform, for come nigh hath the reign of the heavens,'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for this is he who was spoken of by Isaiah the prophet, saying, 'A voice of one crying in the wilderness, Prepare ye the way of the Lord, straight make ye His paths.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And this John had his clothing of camel's hair, and a girdle of skin round his loins, and his nourishment was locusts and honey of the field.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then were going forth unto him Jerusalem, and all Judea, and all the region round about the Jordan,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they were baptized in the Jordan by him, confessing their sins.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having seen many of the Pharisees and Sadducees coming about his baptism, he said to them, 'Brood of vipers! who did shew you to flee from the coming wrath?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: bear, therefore, fruits worthy of the reformation,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and do not think to say in yourselves, A father we have -- Abraham, for I say to you, that God is able out of these stones to raise children to Abraham,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and now also, the axe unto the root of the trees is laid, every tree therefore not bearing good fruit is hewn down, and to fire is cast.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'I indeed do baptize you with water to reformation, but he who after me is coming is mightier than I, of whom I am not worthy to bear the sandals, he shall baptize you with the Holy Spirit and with fire,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: whose fan [is] in his hand, and he will thoroughly cleanse his floor, and will gather his wheat to the storehouse, but the chaff he will burn with fire unquenchable.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then cometh Jesus from Galilee upon the Jordan, unto John to be baptized by him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but John was forbidding him, saying, 'I have need by thee to be baptized -- and thou dost come unto me!'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: But Jesus answering said to him, 'Suffer now, for thus it is becoming to us to fulfil all righteousness,' then he doth suffer him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having been baptized, Jesus went up immediately from the water, and lo, opened to him were the heavens, and he saw the Spirit of God descending as a dove, and coming upon him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and lo, a voice out of the heavens, saying, 'This is My Son -- the Beloved, in whom I did delight.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then Jesus was led up to the wilderness by the Spirit, to be tempted by the Devil,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having fasted forty days and forty nights, afterwards he did hunger.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the Tempter having come to him said, 'If Son thou art of God -- speak that these stones may become loaves.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: But he answering said, 'It hath been written, Not upon bread alone doth man live, but upon every word coming forth from the mouth of God.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then doth the Devil take him to the [holy] city, and doth set him on the pinnacle of the temple,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and saith to him, 'If Son thou art of God -- cast thyself down, for it hath been written, that, His messengers He shall charge concerning thee, and on hands they shall bear thee up, that thou mayest not dash on a stone thy foot.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jesus said to him again, 'It hath been written, Thou shalt not tempt the Lord thy God.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Again doth the Devil take him to a very high mount, and doth shew to him all the kingdoms of the world and the glory of them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and saith to him, 'All these to thee I will give, if falling down thou mayest bow to me.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then saith Jesus to him, 'Go -- Adversary, for it hath been written, The Lord thy God thou shalt bow to, and Him only thou shalt serve.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then doth the Devil leave him, and lo, messengers came and were ministering to him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus having heard that John was delivered up, did withdraw to Galilee,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having left Nazareth, having come, he dwelt at Capernaum that is by the sea, in the borders of Zebulun and Naphtalim,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: that it might be fulfilled that was spoken through Isaiah the prophet, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Land of Zebulun and land of Naphtali, way of the sea, beyond the Jordan, Galilee of the nations! --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the people that is sitting in darkness saw a great light, and to those sitting in a region and shadow of death -- light arose to them.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: From that time began Jesus to proclaim and to say, 'Reform ye, for come nigh hath the reign of the heavens.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus, walking by the sea of Galilee, saw two brothers, Simon named Peter and Andrew his brother, casting a drag into the sea -- for they were fishers --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he saith to them, 'Come ye after me, and I will make you fishers of men,'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they, immediately, having left the nets, did follow him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having advanced thence, he saw other two brothers, James of Zebedee, and John his brother, in the boat with Zebedee their father, refitting their nets, and he called them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they, immediately, having left the boat and their father, did follow him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus was going about all Galilee teaching in their synagogues, and proclaiming the good news of the reign, and healing every disease, and every malady among the people,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and his fame went forth to all Syria, and they brought to him all having ailments, pressed with manifold sicknesses and pains, and demoniacs, and lunatics, and paralytics, and he healed them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And there followed him many multitudes from Galilee, and Decapolis, and Jerusalem, and Judea, and beyond the Jordan.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having seen the multitudes, he went up to the mount, and he having sat down, his disciples came to him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having opened his mouth, he was teaching them, saying:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Happy the poor in spirit -- because theirs is the reign of the heavens.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Happy the mourning -- because they shall be comforted.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Happy the meek -- because they shall inherit the land.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Happy those hungering and thirsting for righteousness -- because they shall be filled.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Happy the kind -- because they shall find kindness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Happy the clean in heart -- because they shall see God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Happy the peacemakers -- because they shall be called Sons of God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Happy those persecuted for righteousness' sake -- because theirs is the reign of the heavens.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Happy are ye whenever they may reproach you, and may persecute, and may say any evil thing against you falsely for my sake --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: rejoice ye and be glad, because your reward [is] great in the heavens, for thus did they persecute the prophets who were before you.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Ye are the salt of the land, but if the salt may lose savour, in what shall it be salted? for nothing is it good henceforth, except to be cast without, and to be trodden down by men.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Ye are the light of the world, a city set upon a mount is not able to be hid;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: nor do they light a lamp, and put it under the measure, but on the lamp-stand, and it shineth to all those in the house;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: so let your light shine before men, that they may see your good works, and may glorify your Father who [is] in the heavens.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Do not suppose that I came to throw down the law or the prophets -- I did not come to throw down, but to fulfil;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for, verily I say to you, till that the heaven and the earth may pass away, one iota or one tittle may not pass away from the law, till that all may come to pass.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Whoever therefore may loose one of these commands -- the least -- and may teach men so, least he shall be called in the reign of the heavens, but whoever may do and may teach [them], he shall be called great in the reign of the heavens.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'For I say to you, that if your righteousness may not abound above that of the scribes and Pharisees, ye may not enter to the reign of the heavens.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Ye heard that it was said to the ancients: Thou shalt not kill, and whoever may kill shall be in danger of the judgment;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but I -- I say to you, that every one who is angry at his brother without cause, shall be in danger of the judgment, and whoever may say to his brother, Empty fellow! shall be in danger of the sanhedrim, and whoever may say, Rebel! shall be in danger of the gehenna of the fire.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'If, therefore, thou mayest bring thy gift to the altar, and there mayest remember that thy brother hath anything against thee,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: leave there thy gift before the altar, and go -- first be reconciled to thy brother, and then having come bring thy gift.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Be agreeing with thy opponent quickly, while thou art in the way with him, that the opponent may not deliver thee to the judge, and the judge may deliver thee to the officer, and to prison thou mayest be cast,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: verily I say to thee, thou mayest not come forth thence till that thou mayest pay the last farthing.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Ye heard that it was said to the ancients: Thou shalt not commit adultery;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but I -- I say to you, that every one who is looking on a woman to desire her, did already commit adultery with her in his heart.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'But, if thy right eye doth cause thee to stumble, pluck it out and cast from thee, for it is good to thee that one of thy members may perish, and not thy whole body be cast to gehenna.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And, if thy right hand doth cause thee to stumble, cut it off, and cast from thee, for it is good to thee that one of thy members may perish, and not thy whole body be cast to gehenna.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And it was said, That whoever may put away his wife, let him give to her a writing of divorce;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but I -- I say to you, that whoever may put away his wife, save for the matter of whoredom, doth make her to commit adultery; and whoever may marry her who hath been put away doth commit adultery.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Again, ye heard that it was said to the ancients: Thou shalt not swear falsely, but thou shalt pay to the Lord thine oaths;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but I -- I say to you, not to swear at all; neither by the heaven, because it is the throne of God,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: nor by the earth, because it is His footstool, nor by Jerusalem, because it is a city of a great king,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: nor by thy head mayest thou swear, because thou art not able one hair to make white or black;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but let your word be, Yes, Yes, No, No, and that which is more than these is of the evil.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Ye heard that it was said: Eye for eye, and tooth for tooth;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but I -- I say to you, not to resist the evil, but whoever shall slap thee on thy right cheek, turn to him also the other;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and whoever is willing to take thee to law, and thy coat to take -- suffer to him also the cloak.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And whoever shall impress thee one mile, go with him two,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: to him who is asking of thee be giving, and him who is willing to borrow from thee thou mayest not turn away.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Ye heard that it was said: Thou shalt love thy neighbour, and shalt hate thine enemy;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but I -- I say to you, Love your enemies, bless those cursing you, do good to those hating you, and pray for those accusing you falsely, and persecuting you,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: that ye may be sons of your Father in the heavens, because His sun He doth cause to rise on evil and good, and He doth send rain on righteous and unrighteous.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'For, if ye may love those loving you, what reward have ye? do not also the tax-gatherers the same?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if ye may salute your brethren only, what do ye abundant? do not also the tax-gatherers so?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: ye shall therefore be perfect, as your Father who [is] in the heavens is perfect.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Take heed your kindness not to do before men, to be seen by them, and if not -- reward ye have not from your Father who [is] in the heavens;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: whenever, therefore, thou mayest do kindness, thou mayest not sound a trumpet before thee as the hypocrites do, in the synagogues, and in the streets, that they may have glory from men; verily I say to you -- they have their reward!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'But thou, doing kindness, let not thy left hand know what thy right hand doth,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: that thy kindness may be in secret, and thy Father who is seeing in secret Himself shall reward thee manifestly.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And when thou mayest pray, thou shalt not be as the hypocrites, because they love in the synagogues, and in the corners of the broad places -- standing -- to pray, that they may be seen of men; verily I say to you, that they have their reward.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'But thou, when thou mayest pray, go into thy chamber, and having shut thy door, pray to thy Father who [is] in secret, and thy Father who is seeing in secret, shall reward thee manifestly.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And -- praying -- ye may not use vain repetitions like the nations, for they think that in their much speaking they shall be heard,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: be ye not therefore like to them, for your Father doth know those things that ye have need of before your asking him;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: thus therefore pray ye: 'Our Father who [art] in the heavens! hallowed be Thy name.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Thy reign come: Thy will come to pass, as in heaven also on the earth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Our appointed bread give us to-day.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And forgive us our debts, as also we forgive our debtors.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And mayest Thou not lead us to temptation, but deliver us from the evil, because Thine is the reign, and the power, and the glory -- to the ages. Amen.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'For, if ye may forgive men their trespasses He also will forgive you -- your Father who [is] in the heavens;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but if ye may not forgive men their trespasses, neither will your Father forgive your trespasses.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And when ye may fast, be ye not as the hypocrites, of sour countenances, for they disfigure their faces, that they may appear to men fasting; verily I say to you, that they have their reward.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'But thou, fasting, anoint thy head, and wash thy face,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: that thou mayest not appear to men fasting, but to thy Father who [is] in secret, and thy Father, who is seeing in secret, shall reward thee manifestly.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Treasure not up to yourselves treasures on the earth, where moth and rust disfigure, and where thieves break through and steal,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but treasure up to yourselves treasures in heaven, where neither moth nor rust doth disfigure, and where thieves do not break through nor steal,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for where your treasure is, there will be also your heart.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'The lamp of the body is the eye, if, therefore, thine eye may be perfect, all thy body shall be enlightened,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but if thine eye may be evil, all thy body shall be dark; if, therefore, the light that [is] in thee is darkness -- the darkness, how great!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'None is able to serve two lords, for either he will hate the one and love the other, or he will hold to the one, and despise the other; ye are not able to serve God and Mammon.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Because of this I say to you, be not anxious for your life, what ye may eat, and what ye may drink, nor for your body, what ye may put on. Is not the life more than the nourishment, and the body than the clothing?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: look to the fowls of the heaven, for they do not sow, nor reap, nor gather into storehouses, and your heavenly Father doth nourish them; are not ye much better than they?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And who of you, being anxious, is able to add to his age one cubit?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and about clothing why are ye anxious? consider well the lilies of the field; how do they grow? they do not labour, nor do they spin;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I say to you, that not even Solomon in all his glory was arrayed as one of these.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And if the herb of the field, that to-day is, and to-morrow is cast to the furnace, God doth so clothe -- not much more you, O ye of little faith?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: therefore ye may not be anxious, saying, What may we eat? or, What may we drink? or, What may we put round?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for all these do the nations seek for, for your heavenly Father doth know that ye have need of all these;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but seek ye first the reign of God and His righteousness, and all these shall be added to you.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Be not therefore anxious for the morrow, for the morrow shall be anxious for its own things; sufficient for the day [is] the evil of it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Judge not, that ye may not be judged,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for in what judgment ye judge, ye shall be judged, and in what measure ye measure, it shall be measured to you.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And why dost thou behold the mote that [is] in thy brother's eye, and the beam that [is] in thine own eye dost not consider?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: or, how wilt thou say to thy brother, Suffer I may cast out the mote from thine eye, and lo, the beam [is] in thine own eye?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Hypocrite, cast out first the beam out of thine own eye, and then thou shalt see clearly to cast out the mote out of thy brother's eye.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Ye may not give that which is [holy] to the dogs, nor cast your pearls before the swine, that they may not trample them among their feet, and having turned -- may rend you.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Ask, and it shall be given to you; seek, and ye shall find; knock, and it shall be opened to you;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for every one who is asking doth receive, and he who is seeking doth find, and to him who is knocking it shall be opened.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Or what man is of you, of whom, if his son may ask a loaf -- a stone will he present to him?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if a fish he may ask -- a serpent will he present to him?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: if, therefore, ye being evil, have known good gifts to give to your children, how much more shall your Father who [is] in the heavens give good things to those asking him?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'All things, therefore, whatever ye may will that men may be doing to you, so also do to them, for this is the law and the prophets.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Go ye in through the strait gate, because wide [is] the gate, and broad the way that is leading to the destruction, and many are those going in through it;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: how strait [is] the gate, and compressed the way that is leading to the life, and few are those finding it!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'But, take heed of the false prophets, who come unto you in sheep's clothing, and inwardly are ravening wolves.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: From their fruits ye shall know them; do [men] gather from thorns grapes? or from thistles figs?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: so every good tree doth yield good fruits, but the bad tree doth yield evil fruits.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A good tree is not able to yield evil fruits, nor a bad tree to yield good fruits.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Every tree not yielding good fruit is cut down and is cast to fire:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: therefore from their fruits ye shall know them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Not every one who is saying to me Lord, lord, shall come into the reign of the heavens; but he who is doing the will of my Father who is in the heavens.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Many will say to me in that day, Lord, lord, have we not in thy name prophesied? and in thy name cast out demons? and in thy name done many mighty things?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and then I will acknowledge to them, that -- I never knew you, depart from me ye who are working lawlessness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Therefore, every one who doth hear of me these words, and doth do them, I will liken him to a wise man who built his house upon the rock;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the rain did descend, and the streams came, and the winds blew, and they beat on that house, and it fell not, for it had been founded on the rock.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And every one who is hearing of me these words, and is not doing them, shall be likened to a foolish man who built his house upon the sand;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the rain did descend, and the streams came, and the winds blew, and they beat on that house, and it fell, and its fall was great.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass, when Jesus ended these words, the multitudes were astonished at his teaching,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for he was teaching them as having authority, and not as the scribes.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And when he came down from the mount, great multitudes did follow him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and lo, a leper having come, was bowing to him, saying, 'Sir, if thou art willing, thou art able to cleanse me;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having stretched forth the hand, Jesus touched him, saying, 'I will, be thou cleansed,' and immediately his leprosy was cleansed.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus saith to him, 'See, thou mayest tell no one, but go, thyself shew to the priest, and bring the gift that Moses commanded for a testimony to them.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus having entered into Capernaum, there came to him a centurion calling upon him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and saying, 'Sir, my young man hath been laid in the house a paralytic, fearfully afflicted,'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jesus saith to him, 'I, having come, will heal him.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the centurion answering said, 'Sir, I am not worthy that thou mayest enter under my roof, but only say a word, and my servant shall be healed;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for I also am a man under authority, having under myself soldiers, and I say to this one, Go, and he goeth, and to another, Be coming, and he cometh, and to my servant, Do this, and he doth [it].'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus having heard, did wonder, and said to those following, 'Verily I say to you, not even in Israel so great faith have I found;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I say to you, that many from east and west shall come and recline (at meat) with Abraham, and Isaac, and Jacob, in the reign of the heavens,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but the sons of the reign shall be cast forth to the outer darkness -- there shall be the weeping and the gnashing of the teeth.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus said to the centurion, 'Go, and as thou didst believe let it be to thee;' and his young man was healed in that hour.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus having come into the house of Peter, saw his mother-in-law laid, and fevered,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he touched her hand, and the fever left her, and she arose, and was ministering to them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And evening having come, they brought to him many demoniacs, and he did cast out the spirits with a word, and did heal all who were ill,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: that it might be fulfilled that was spoken through Isaiah the prophet, saying, 'Himself took our infirmities, and the sicknesses he did bear.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus having seen great multitudes about him, did command to depart to the other side;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and a certain scribe having come, said to him, 'Teacher, I will follow thee wherever thou mayest go;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jesus saith to him, 'The foxes have holes, and the birds of the heaven places of rest, but the Son of Man hath not where he may lay the head.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And another of his disciples said to him, 'Sir, permit me first to depart and to bury my father;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jesus said to him, 'Follow me, and suffer the dead to bury their own dead.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And when he entered into the boat his disciples did follow him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and lo, a great tempest arose in the sea, so that the boat was being covered by the waves, but he was sleeping,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and his disciples having come to him, awoke him, saying, 'Sir, save us; we are perishing.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he saith to them, 'Why are ye fearful, O ye of little faith?' Then having risen, he rebuked the winds and the sea, and there was a great calm;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the men wondered, saying, 'What kind -- is this, that even the wind and the sea do obey him?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he having come to the other side, to the region of the Gergesenes, there met him two demoniacs, coming forth out of the tombs, very fierce, so that no one was able to pass over by that way,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and lo, they cried out, saying, 'What -- to us and to thee, Jesus, Son of God? didst thou come hither, before the time, to afflict us?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And there was far off from them a herd of many swine feeding,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the demons were calling on him, saying, 'If thou dost cast us forth, permit us to go away to the herd of the swine;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he saith to them, 'Go.' And having come forth, they went to the herd of the swine, and lo, the whole herd of the swine rushed down the steep, to the sea, and died in the waters,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and those feeding did flee, and, having gone to the city, they declared all, and the matter of the demoniacs.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And lo, all the city came forth to meet Jesus, and having seen him, they called on [him] that he might depart from their borders.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having gone to the boat, he passed over, and came to his own city,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and lo, they were bringing to him a paralytic, laid upon a couch, and Jesus having seen their faith, said to the paralytic, 'Be of good courage, child, thy sins have been forgiven thee.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And lo, certain of the scribes said within themselves, 'This one doth speak evil.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus, having known their thoughts, said, 'Why think ye evil in your hearts?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for which is easier? to say, The sins have been forgiven to thee; or to say, Rise, and walk?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'But, that ye may know that the Son of Man hath power upon the earth to forgive sins -- (then saith he to the paralytic) -- having risen, take up thy couch, and go to thy house.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he, having risen, went to his house,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the multitudes having seen, wondered, and glorified God, who did give such power to men.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus passing by thence, saw a man sitting at the tax-office, named Matthew, and saith to him, 'Be following me,' and he, having risen, did follow him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass, he reclining (at meat) in the house, that lo, many tax-gatherers and sinners having come, were lying (at meat) with Jesus and his disciples,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the Pharisees having seen, said to his disciples, 'Wherefore with the tax-gatherers and sinners doth your teacher eat?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus having heard, said to them, 'They who are whole have no need of a physician, but they who are ill;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but having gone, learn ye what is, Kindness I will, and not sacrifice, for I did not come to call righteous men, but sinners, to reformation.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then come to him do the disciples of John, saying, 'Wherefore do we and the Pharisees fast much, and thy disciples fast not?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus said to them, 'Can the sons of the bride-chamber mourn, so long as the bridegroom is with them? but days shall come when the bridegroom may be taken from them, and then they shall fast.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And no one doth put a patch of undressed cloth on an old garment, for its filling up doth take from the garment, and a worse rent is made.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Nor do they put new wine into old skins, and if not -- the skins burst, and the wine doth run out, and the skins are destroyed, but they put new wine into new skins, and both are preserved together.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: While he is speaking these things to them, lo, a ruler having come, was bowing to him, saying that 'My daughter just now died, but, having come, lay thy hand upon her, and she shall live.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus having risen, did follow him, also his disciples,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and lo, a woman having an issue of blood twelve years, having come to him behind, did touch the fringe of his garments,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for she said within herself, 'If only I may touch his garment, I shall be saved.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus having turned about, and having seen her, said, 'Be of good courage, daughter, thy faith hath saved thee,' and the woman was saved from that hour.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus having come to the house of the ruler, and having seen the minstrels and the multitude making tumult,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: he saith to them, 'Withdraw, for the damsel did not die, but doth sleep,' and they were deriding him;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but, when the multitude was put forth, having gone in, he took hold of her hand, and the damsel arose,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the fame of this went forth to all the land.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus passing on thence, two blind men followed him, calling and saying, 'Deal kindly with us, Son of David.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he having come to the house, the blind men came to him, and Jesus saith to them, 'Believe ye that I am able to do this?' They say to him, 'Yes, sir.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then touched he their eyes, saying, 'According to your faith let it be to you,'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and their eyes were opened, and Jesus strictly charged them, saying, 'See, let no one know;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but they, having gone forth, did spread his fame in all that land.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And as they are coming forth, lo, they brought to him a man dumb, a demoniac,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the demon having been cast out, the dumb spake, and the multitude did wonder, saying that 'It was never so seen in Israel:'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but the Pharisees said, 'By the ruler of the demons he doth cast out the demons.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus was going up and down all the cities and the villages, teaching in their synagogues, and proclaiming the good news of the reign, and healing every sickness and every malady among the people.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having seen the multitudes, he was moved with compassion for them, that they were faint and cast aside, as sheep not having a shepherd,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then saith he to his disciples, 'The harvest indeed [is] abundant, but the workmen few;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: beseech ye therefore the Lord of the harvest, that he may put forth workmen to His harvest.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having called to him his twelve disciples, he gave to them power over unclean spirits, so as to be casting them out, and to be healing every sickness, and every malady.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And of the twelve apostles the names are these: first, Simon, who is called Peter, and Andrew his brother; James of Zebedee, and John his brother;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Philip, and Bartholomew; Thomas, and Matthew the tax-gatherer; James of Alpheus, and Lebbeus who was surnamed Thaddeus;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Simon the Cananite, and Judas Iscariot, who did also deliver him up.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These twelve did Jesus send forth, having given command to them, saying, 'To the way of the nations go not away, and into a city of the Samaritans go not in,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and be going rather unto the lost sheep of the house of Israel.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And, going on, proclaim saying that, the reign of the heavens hath come nigh;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: infirm ones be healing, lepers be cleansing, dead be raising, demons be casting out -- freely ye did receive, freely give.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Provide not gold, nor silver, nor brass in your girdles,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: nor scrip for the way, nor two coats, nor sandals, nor staff -- for the workman is worthy of his nourishment.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And into whatever city or village ye may enter, inquire ye who in it is worthy, and there abide, till ye may go forth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And coming to the house salute it,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if indeed the house be worthy, let your peace come upon it; and if it be not worthy, let your peace turn back to you.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And whoever may not receive you nor hear your words, coming forth from that house or city, shake off the dust of your feet,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: verily I say to you, It shall be more tolerable for the land of Sodom and Gomorrah in the day of judgment than for that city.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Lo, I do send you forth as sheep in the midst of wolves, be ye therefore wise as the serpents, and simple as the doves.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And, take ye heed of men, for they will give you up to sanhedrims, and in their synagogues they will scourge you,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and before governors and kings ye shall be brought for my sake, for a testimony to them and to the nations.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And whenever they may deliver you up, be not anxious how or what ye may speak, for it shall be given you in that hour what ye shall speak;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for ye are not the speakers, but the Spirit of your Father that is speaking in you.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And brother shall deliver up brother to death, and father child, and children shall rise up against parents, and shall put them to death,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye shall be hated by all because of my name, but he who hath endured to the end, he shall be saved.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And whenever they may persecute you in this city, flee to the other, for verily I say to you, ye may not have completed the cities of Israel till the Son of Man may come.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'A disciple is not above the teacher, nor a servant above his lord;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: sufficient to the disciple that he may be as his teacher, and the servant as his lord; if the master of the house they did call Beelzeboul, how much more those of his household?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Ye may not, therefore, fear them, for there is nothing covered, that shall not be revealed, and hid, that shall not be known;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: that which I tell you in the darkness, speak in the light, and that which you hear at the ear, proclaim on the house-tops.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And be not afraid of those killing the body, and are not able to kill the soul, but fear rather Him who is able both soul and body to destroy in gehenna.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Are not two sparrows sold for an assar? and one of them shall not fall on the ground without your Father;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and of you -- even the hairs of the head are all numbered;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: be not therefore afraid, than many sparrows ye are better.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Every one, therefore, who shall confess in me before men, I also will confess in him before my Father who is in the heavens;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and whoever shall deny me before men, I also will deny him before my Father who is in the heavens.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Ye may not suppose that I came to put peace on the earth; I did not come to put peace, but a sword;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for I came to set a man at variance against his father, and a daughter against her mother, and a daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the enemies of a man are those of his household.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'He who is loving father or mother above me, is not worthy of me, and he who is loving son or daughter above me, is not worthy of me,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and whoever doth not receive his cross and follow after me, is not worthy of me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'He who found his life shall lose it, and he who lost his life for my sake shall find it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'He who is receiving you doth receive me, and he who is receiving me doth receive Him who sent me,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: he who is receiving a prophet in the name of a prophet, shall receive a prophet's reward, and he who is receiving a righteous man in the name of a righteous man, shall receive a righteous man's reward,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and whoever may give to drink to one of these little ones a cup of cold water only in the name of a disciple, verily I say to you, he may not lose his reward.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass, when Jesus ended directing his twelve disciples, he departed thence to teach and to preach in their cities.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And John having heard in the prison the works of the Christ, having sent two of his disciples,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: said to him, 'Art thou He who is coming, or for another do we look?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus answering said to them, 'Having gone, declare to John the things that ye hear and see,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: blind receive sight, and lame walk, lepers are cleansed, and deaf hear, dead are raised, and poor have good news proclaimed,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and happy is he who may not be stumbled in me.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And as they are going, Jesus began to say to the multitudes concerning John, 'What went ye out to the wilderness to view? -- a reed shaken by the wind?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'But what went ye out to see? -- a man clothed in soft garments? lo, those wearing the soft things are in the kings' houses.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'But what went ye out to see? -- a prophet? yes, I say to you, and more than a prophet,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for this is he of whom it hath been written, Lo, I do send My messenger before thy face, who shall prepare thy way before thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Verily I say to you, there hath not risen, among those born of women, a greater than John the Baptist, but he who is least in the reign of the heavens is greater than he.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And, from the days of John the Baptist till now, the reign of the heavens doth suffer violence, and violent men do take it by force,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for all the prophets and the law till John did prophesy,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if ye are willing to receive [it], he is Elijah who was about to come;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: he who is having ears to hear -- let him hear.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And to what shall I liken this generation? it is like little children in market-places, sitting and calling to their comrades,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and saying, We piped unto you, and ye did not dance, we lamented to you, and ye did not smite the breast.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, He hath a demon;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the Son of Man came eating and drinking, and they say, Lo, a man, a glutton, and a wine-drinker, a friend of tax-gatherers and sinners, and wisdom was justified of her children.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then began he to reproach the cities in which were done most of his mighty works, because they did not reform.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Woe to thee, Chorazin! woe to thee, Bethsaida! because, if in Tyre and Sidon had been done the mighty works that were done in you, long ago in sackcloth and ashes they had reformed;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but I say to you, to Tyre and Sidon it shall be more tolerable in a day of judgment than for you.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And thou, Capernaum, which unto the heaven wast exalted, unto hades shalt be brought down, because if in Sodom had been done the mighty works that were done in thee, it had remained unto this day;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but I say to you, to the land of Sodom it shall be more tolerable in a day of judgment than to thee.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: At that time Jesus answering said, 'I do confess to Thee, Father, Lord of the heavens and of the earth, that thou didst hide these things from wise and understanding ones, and didst reveal them to babes.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Yes, Father, because so it was good pleasure before Thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'All things were delivered to me by my Father, and none doth know the Son, except the Father, nor doth any know the Father, except the Son, and he to whom the Son may wish to reveal [Him].

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Come unto me, all ye labouring and burdened ones, and I will give you rest,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: take up my yoke upon you, and learn from me, because I am meek and humble in heart, and ye shall find rest to your souls,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for my yoke [is] easy, and my burden is light.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: At that time did Jesus go on the sabbaths through the corn, and his disciples were hungry, and they began to pluck ears, and to eat,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the Pharisees having seen, said to him, 'Lo, thy disciples do that which it is not lawful to do on a sabbath.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he said to them, 'Did ye not read what David did, when he was hungry, himself and those with him --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: how he went into the house of God, and the loaves of the presentation did eat, which it is not lawful to him to eat, nor to those with him, except to the priests alone?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Or did ye not read in the Law, that on the sabbaths the priests in the temple do profane the sabbath, and are blameless?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I say to you, that a greater than the temple is here;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if ye had known what is: Kindness I will, and not sacrifice -- ye had not condemned the blameless,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for the son of man is lord even of the sabbath.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having departed thence, he went to their synagogue,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and lo, there was a man having the hand withered, and they questioned him, saying, 'Is it lawful to heal on the sabbaths?' that they might accuse him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he said to them, 'What man shall be of you, who shall have one sheep, and if this may fall on the sabbaths into a ditch, will not lay hold on it and raise [it]?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: How much better, therefore, is a man than a sheep? -- so that it is lawful on the sabbaths to do good.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then saith he to the man, 'Stretch forth thy hand,' and he stretched [it] forth, and it was restored whole as the other.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the Pharisees having gone forth, held a consultation against him, how they might destroy him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jesus having known, withdrew thence, and there followed him great multitudes, and he healed them all,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and did charge them that they might not make him manifest,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: that it might be fulfilled that was spoken through Isaiah the prophet, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Lo, My servant, whom I did choose, My beloved, in whom My soul did delight, I will put My Spirit upon him, and judgment to the nations he shall declare,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: he shall not strive nor cry, nor shall any hear in the broad places his voice,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: a bruised reed he shall not break, and smoking flax he shall not quench, till he may put forth judgment to victory,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and in his name shall nations hope.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then was brought to him a demoniac, blind and dumb, and he healed him, so that the blind and dumb both spake and saw.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And all the multitudes were amazed, and said, 'Is this the Son of David?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but the Pharisees having heard, said, 'This one doth not cast out demons, except by Beelzeboul, ruler of the demons.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus, knowing their thoughts, said to them, 'Every kingdom having been divided against itself is desolated, and no city or house having been divided against itself, doth stand,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if the Adversary doth cast out the Adversary, against himself he was divided, how then doth his kingdom stand?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And if I, by Beelzeboul, do cast out the demons, your sons -- by whom do they cast out? because of this they -- they shall be your judges.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'But if I, by the Spirit of God, do cast out the demons, then come already unto you did the reign of God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Or how is one able to go into the house of the strong man, and to plunder his goods, if first he may not bind the strong man? and then his house he will plunder.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'He who is not with me is against me, and he who is not gathering with me, doth scatter.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Because of this I say to you, all sin and evil speaking shall be forgiven to men, but the evil speaking of the Spirit shall not be forgiven to men.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And whoever may speak a word against the Son of Man it shall be forgiven to him, but whoever may speak against the Holy Spirit, it shall not be forgiven him, neither in this age, nor in that which is coming.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Either make the tree good, and its fruit good, or make the tree bad, and its fruit bad, for from the fruit is the tree known.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Brood of vipers! how are ye able to speak good things -- being evil? for out of the abundance of the heart doth the mouth speak.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The good man out of the good treasure of the heart doth put forth the good things, and the evil man out of the evil treasure doth put forth evil things.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And I say to you, that every idle word that men may speak, they shall give for it a reckoning in a day of judgment;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for from thy words thou shalt be declared righteous, and from thy words thou shalt be declared unrighteous.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then answered certain of the scribes and Pharisees, saying, 'Teacher, we will to see a sign from thee.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he answering said to them, 'A generation, evil and adulterous, doth seek a sign, and a sign shall not be given to it, except the sign of Jonah the prophet;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for, as Jonah was in the belly of the fish three days and three nights, so shall the Son of Man be in the heart of the earth three days and three nights.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Men of Nineveh shall stand up in the judgment with this generation, and shall condemn it, for they reformed at the proclamation of Jonah, and lo, a greater than Jonah here!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'A queen of the south shall rise up in the judgment with this generation, and shall condemn it, for she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon, and lo, a greater than Solomon here!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And, when the unclean spirit may go forth from the man, it doth walk through dry places seeking rest, and doth not find;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then it saith, I will turn back to my house whence I came forth; and having come, it findeth [it] unoccupied, swept, and adorned:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then doth it go, and take with itself seven other spirits more evil than itself, and having gone in they dwell there, and the last of that man doth become worse than the first; so shall it be also to this evil generation.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And while he was yet speaking to the multitudes, lo, his mother and brethren had stood without, seeking to speak to him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and one said to him, 'Lo, thy mother and thy brethren do stand without, seeking to speak to thee.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he answering said to him who spake to him, 'Who is my mother? and who are my brethren?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having stretched forth his hand toward his disciples, he said, 'Lo, my mother and my brethren!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for whoever may do the will of my Father who is in the heavens, he is my brother, and sister, and mother.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And in that day Jesus, having gone forth from the house, was sitting by the sea,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and gathered together unto him were many multitudes, so that he having gone into the boat did sit down, and all the multitude on the beach did stand,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he spake to them many things in similes, saying: 'Lo, the sower went forth to sow,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and in his sowing, some indeed fell by the way, and the fowls did come and devour them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and others fell upon the rocky places, where they had not much earth, and immediately they sprang forth, through not having depth of earth,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the sun having risen they were scorched, and through not having root, they withered,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and others fell upon the thorns, and the thorns did come up and choke them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and others fell upon the good ground, and were giving fruit, some indeed a hundredfold, and some sixty, and some thirty.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He who is having ears to hear -- let him hear.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the disciples having come near, said to him, 'Wherefore in similes dost thou speak to them?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he answering said to them that -- 'To you it hath been given to know the secrets of the reign of the heavens, and to these it hath not been given,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for whoever hath, it shall be given to him, and he shall have overabundance, and whoever hath not, even that which he hath shall be taken from him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Because of this, in similes do I speak to them, because seeing they do not see, and hearing they do not hear, nor understand,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and fulfilled on them is the prophecy of Isaiah, that saith, With hearing ye shall hear, and ye shall not understand, and seeing ye shall see, and ye shall not perceive,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for made gross was the heart of this people, and with the ears they heard heavily, and their eyes they did close, lest they might see with the eyes, and with the ears might hear, and with the heart understand, and turn back, and I might heal them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And happy are your eyes because they see, and your ears because they hear,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for verily I say to you, that many prophets and righteous men did desire to see that which ye look on, and they did not see, and to hear that which ye hear, and they did not hear.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Ye, therefore, hear ye the simile of the sower:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Every one hearing the word of the reign, and not understanding -- the evil one doth come, and doth catch that which hath been sown in his heart; this is that sown by the way.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And that sown on the rocky places, this is he who is hearing the word, and immediately with joy is receiving it,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he hath not root in himself, but is temporary, and persecution or tribulation having happened because of the word, immediately he is stumbled.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And that sown toward the thorns, this is he who is hearing the word, and the anxiety of this age, and the deceitfulness of the riches, do choke the word, and it becometh unfruitful.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And that sown on the good ground: this is he who is hearing the word, and is understanding, who indeed doth bear fruit, and doth make, some indeed a hundredfold, and some sixty, and some thirty.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Another simile he set before them, saying: 'The reign of the heavens was likened to a man sowing good seed in his field,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and, while men are sleeping, his enemy came and sowed darnel in the midst of the wheat, and went away,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and when the herb sprang up, and yielded fruit, then appeared also the darnel.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And the servants of the householder, having come near, said to him, Sir, good seed didst thou not sow in thy field? whence then hath it the darnel?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he saith to them, A man, an enemy, did this; and the servants said to him, Wilt thou, then, [that] having gone away we may gather it up?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And he said, No, lest -- gathering up the darnel -- ye root up with it the wheat,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: suffer both to grow together till the harvest, and in the time of the harvest I will say to the reapers, Gather up first the darnel, and bind it in bundles, to burn it, and the wheat gather up into my storehouse.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Another simile he set before them, saying: 'The reign of the heavens is like to a grain of mustard, which a man having taken, did sow in his field,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: which less, indeed, is than all the seeds, but when it may be grown, is greatest of the herbs, and becometh a tree, so that the birds of the heaven do come and rest in its branches.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Another simile spake he to them: 'The reign of the heavens is like to leaven, which a woman having taken, hid in three measures of meal, till the whole was leavened.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
