translations.text: All these things spake Jesus in similes to the multitudes, and without a simile he was not speaking to them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: that it might be fulfilled that was spoken through the prophet, saying, 'I will open in similes my mouth, I will utter things having been hidden from the foundation of the world.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then having let away the multitudes, Jesus came to the house, and his disciples came near to him, saying, 'Explain to us the simile of the darnel of the field.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he answering said to them, 'He who is sowing the good seed is the Son of Man,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the field is the world, and the good seed, these are the sons of the reign, and the darnel are the sons of the evil one,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the enemy who sowed them is the devil, and the harvest is a full end of the age, and the reapers are messengers.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'As, then, the darnel is gathered up, and is burned with fire, so shall it be in the full end of this age,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the Son of Man shall send forth his messengers, and they shall gather up out of his kingdom all the stumbling-blocks, and those doing the unlawlessness,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and shall cast them to the furnace of the fire; there shall be the weeping and the gnashing of the teeth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Then shall the righteous shine forth as the sun in the reign of their Father. He who is having ears to hear -- let him hear.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Again, the reign of the heavens is like to treasure hid in the field, which a man having found did hide, and from his joy goeth, and all, as much as he hath, he selleth, and buyeth that field.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Again, the reign of the heavens is like to a man, a merchant, seeking goodly pearls,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: who having found one pearl of great price, having gone away, hath sold all, as much as he had, and bought it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Again, the reign of the heavens is like to a net that was cast into the sea, and did gather together of every kind,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: which, when it was filled, having drawn up again upon the beach, and having sat down, they gathered the good into vessels, and the bad they did cast out,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: so shall it be in the full end of the age, the messengers shall come forth and separate the evil out of the midst of the righteous,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and shall cast them to the furnace of the fire, there shall be the weeping and the gnashing of the teeth.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jesus saith to them, 'Did ye understand all these?' They say to him, 'Yes, sir.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he said to them, 'Because of this every scribe having been discipled in regard to the reign of the heavens, is like to a man, a householder, who doth bring forth out of his treasure things new and old.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass, when Jesus finished these similes, he removed thence,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having come to his own country, he was teaching them in their synagogue, so that they were astonished, and were saying, 'Whence to this one this wisdom and the mighty works?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: is not this the carpenter's son? is not his mother called Mary, and his brethren James, and Joses, and Simon, and Judas?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and his sisters -- are they not all with us? whence, then, to this one all these?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they were stumbled at him. And Jesus said to them, 'A prophet is not without honour except in his own country, and in his own house:'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he did not there many mighty works, because of their unbelief.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: At that time did Herod the tetrarch hear the fame of Jesus,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and said to his servants, 'This is John the Baptist, he did rise from the dead, and because of this the mighty energies are working in him.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For Herod having laid hold on John, did bind him, and did put him in prison, because of Herodias his brother Philip's wife,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for John was saying to him, 'It is not lawful to thee to have her,'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and, willing to kill him, he feared the multitude, because as a prophet they were holding him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: But the birthday of Herod being kept, the daughter of Herodias danced in the midst, and did please Herod,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: whereupon with an oath he professed to give her whatever she might ask.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And she having been instigated by her mother -- 'Give me (says she) here upon a plate the head of John the Baptist;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the king was grieved, but because of the oaths and of those reclining with him, he commanded [it] to be given;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having sent, he beheaded John in the prison,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and his head was brought upon a plate, and was given to the damsel, and she brought [it] nigh to her mother.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And his disciples having come, took up the body, and buried it, and having come, they told Jesus,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jesus having heard, withdrew thence in a boat to a desolate place by himself, and the multitudes having heard did follow him on land from the cities.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus having come forth, saw a great multitude, and was moved with compassion upon them, and did heal their infirm;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and evening having come, his disciples came to him, saying, 'The place is desolate, and the hour hath now past, let away the multitudes that, having gone to the villages, they may buy to themselves food.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus said to them, 'They have no need to go away -- give ye them to eat.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they say to him, 'We have not here except five loaves, and two fishes.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he said, 'Bring ye them to me hither.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having commanded the multitudes to recline upon the grass, and having taken the five loaves and the two fishes, having looked up to the heaven, he did bless, and having broken, he gave the loaves to the disciples, and the disciples to the multitudes,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they did all eat, and were filled, and they took up what was over of the broken pieces twelve hand-baskets full;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and those eating were about five thousand men, apart from women and children.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And immediately Jesus constrained his disciples to go into the boat, and to go before him to the other side, till he might let away the multitudes;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having let away the multitudes, he went up to the mountain by himself to pray, and evening having come, he was there alone,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the boat was now in the midst of the sea, distressed by the waves, for the wind was contrary.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And in the fourth watch of the night Jesus went away to them, walking upon the sea,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the disciples having seen him walking upon the sea, were troubled saying -- 'It is an apparition,' and from the fear they cried out;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and immediately Jesus spake to them, saying, 'Be of good courage, I am [he], be not afraid.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Peter answering him said, 'Sir, if it is thou, bid me come to thee upon the waters;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he said, 'Come;' and having gone down from the boat, Peter walked upon the waters to come unto Jesus,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but seeing the wind vehement, he was afraid, and having begun to sink, he cried out, saying, 'Sir, save me.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And immediately Jesus, having stretched forth the hand, laid hold of him, and saith to him, 'Little faith! for what didst thou waver?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they having gone to the boat the wind lulled,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and those in the boat having come, did bow to him, saying, 'Truly -- God's Son art thou.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having passed over, they came to the land of Gennesaret,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having recognized him, the men of that place sent forth to all that region round about, and they brought to him all who were ill,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and were calling on him that they might only touch the fringe of his garment, and as many as did touch were saved.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then come unto Jesus do they from Jerusalem -- scribes and Pharisees -- saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Wherefore do thy disciples transgress the tradition of the elders? for they do not wash their hands when they may eat bread.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he answering said to them, 'Wherefore also do ye transgress the command of God because of your tradition?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for God did command, saying, Honour thy father and mother; and, He who is speaking evil of father or mother -- let him die the death;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but ye say, Whoever may say to father or mother, An offering [is] whatever thou mayest be profited by me; --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he may not honour his father or his mother, and ye did set aside the command of God because of your tradition.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Hypocrites, well did Isaiah prophesy of you, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: This people doth draw nigh to Me with their mouth, and with the lips it doth honour Me, but their heart is far off from Me;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and in vain do they worship Me, teaching teachings -- commands of men.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having called near the multitude, he said to them, 'Hear and understand:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: not that which is coming into the mouth doth defile the man, but that which is coming forth from the mouth, this defileth the man.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then his disciples having come near, said to him, 'Hast thou known that the Pharisees, having heard the word, were stumbled?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he answering said, 'Every plant that my heavenly Father did not plant shall be rooted up;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: let them alone, guides they are -- blind of blind; and if blind may guide blind, both into a ditch shall fall.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Peter answering said to him, 'Explain to us this simile.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus said, 'Are ye also yet without understanding?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: do ye not understand that all that is going into the mouth doth pass into the belly, and into the drain is cast forth?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but the things coming forth from the mouth from the heart do come forth, and these defile the man;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for out of the heart come forth evil thoughts, murders, adulteries, whoredoms, thefts, false witnessings, evil speakings:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: these are the things defiling the man; but to eat with unwashen hands doth not defile the man.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus having come forth thence, withdrew to the parts of Tyre and Sidon,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and lo, a woman, a Canaanitess, from those borders having come forth, did call to him, saying, 'Deal kindly with me, Sir -- Son of David; my daughter is miserably demonized.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he did not answer her a word; and his disciples having come to him, were asking him, saying -- 'Let her away, because she crieth after us;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he answering said, 'I was not sent except to the lost sheep of the house of Israel.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having come, she was bowing to him, saying, 'Sir, help me;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he answering said, 'It is not good to take the children's bread, and to cast to the little dogs.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And she said, 'Yes, sir, for even the little dogs do eat of the crumbs that are falling from their lords' table;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then answering, Jesus said to her, 'O woman, great [is] thy faith, let it be to thee as thou wilt;' and her daughter was healed from that hour.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus having passed thence, came nigh unto the sea of Galilee, and having gone up to the mountain, he was sitting there,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and there came to him great multitudes, having with them lame, blind, dumb, maimed, and many others, and they did cast them at the feet of Jesus, and he healed them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: so that the multitudes did wonder, seeing dumb ones speaking, maimed whole, lame walking, and blind seeing; and they glorified the God of Israel.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus having called near his disciples, said, 'I have compassion upon the multitude, because now three days they continue with me, and they have not what they may eat; and to let them away fasting I will not, lest they faint in the way.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And his disciples say to him, 'Whence to us, in a wilderness, so many loaves, as to fill so great a multitude?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus saith to them, 'How many loaves have ye?' and they said, 'Seven, and a few little fishes.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he commanded the multitudes to sit down upon the ground,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having taken the seven loaves and the fishes, having given thanks, he did break, and gave to his disciples, and the disciples to the multitude.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they did all eat, and were filled, and they took up what was over of the broken pieces seven baskets full,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and those eating were four thousand men, apart from women and children.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having let away the multitudes, he went into the boat, and did come to the borders of Magdala.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the Pharisees and Sadducees having come, tempting, did question him, to shew to them a sign from the heaven,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he answering said to them, 'Evening having come, ye say, Fair weather, for the heaven is red,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and at morning, Foul weather to-day, for the heaven is red -- gloomy; hypocrites, the face of the heavens indeed ye do know to discern, but the signs of the times ye are not able!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'A generation evil and adulterous doth seek a sign, and a sign shall not be given to it, except the sign of Jonah the prophet;' and having left them he went away.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And his disciples having come to the other side, forgot to take loaves,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jesus said to them, 'Beware, and take heed of the leaven of the Pharisees and Sadducees;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they were reasoning in themselves, saying, 'Because we took no loaves.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus having known, said to them, 'Why reason ye in yourselves, ye of little faith, because ye took no loaves?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: do ye not yet understand, nor remember the five loaves of the five thousand, and how many hand-baskets ye took up?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: nor the seven loaves of the four thousand, and how many baskets ye took up?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: how do ye not understand that I did not speak to you of bread -- to take heed of the leaven of the Pharisees and Sadducees?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then they understood that he did not say to take heed of the leaven of the bread, but of the teaching, of the Pharisees and Sadducees.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus, having come to the parts of Caesarea Philippi, was asking his disciples, saying, 'Who do men say me to be -- the Son of Man?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they said, 'Some, John the Baptist, and others, Elijah, and others, Jeremiah, or one of the prophets.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He saith to them, 'And ye -- who do ye say me to be?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Simon Peter answering said, 'Thou art the Christ, the Son of the living God.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus answering said to him, 'Happy art thou, Simon Bar-Jona, because flesh and blood did not reveal [it] to thee, but my Father who is in the heavens.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And I also say to thee, that thou art a rock, and upon this rock I will build my assembly, and gates of Hades shall not prevail against it;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I will give to thee the keys of the reign of the heavens, and whatever thou mayest bind upon the earth shall be having been bound in the heavens, and whatever thou mayest loose upon the earth shall be having been loosed in the heavens.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then did he charge his disciples that they may say to no one that he is Jesus the Christ.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: From that time began Jesus to shew to his disciples that it is necessary for him to go away to Jerusalem, and to suffer many things from the elders, and chief priests, and scribes, and to be put to death, and the third day to rise.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having taken him aside, Peter began to rebuke him, saying, 'Be kind to thyself, sir; this shall not be to thee;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he having turned, said to Peter, 'Get thee behind me, adversary! thou art a stumbling-block to me, for thou dost not mind the things of God, but the things of men.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then said Jesus to his disciples, 'If any one doth will to come after me, let him disown himself, and take up his cross, and follow me,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for whoever may will to save his life, shall lose it, and whoever may lose his life for my sake shall find it,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for what is a man profited if he may gain the whole world, but of his life suffer loss? or what shall a man give as an exchange for his life?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'For, the Son of Man is about to come in the glory of his Father, with his messengers, and then he will reward each, according to his work.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Verily I say to you, there are certain of those standing here who shall not taste of death till they may see the Son of Man coming in his reign.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And after six days Jesus taketh Peter, and James, and John his brother, and doth bring them up to a high mount by themselves,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he was transfigured before them, and his face shone as the sun, and his garments did become white as the light,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and lo, appear to them did Moses and Elijah, talking together with him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Peter answering said to Jesus, 'Sir, it is good to us to be here; if thou wilt, we may make here three booths -- for thee one, and for Moses one, and one for Elijah.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: While he is yet speaking, lo, a bright cloud overshadowed them, and lo, a voice out of the cloud, saying, 'This is My Son, -- the Beloved, in whom I did delight; hear him.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the disciples having heard, did fall upon their face, and were exceedingly afraid,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jesus having come near, touched them, and said, 'Rise, be not afraid,'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having lifted up their eyes, they saw no one, except Jesus only.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And as they are coming down from the mount, Jesus charged them, saying, 'Say to no one the vision, till the Son of Man out of the dead may rise.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And his disciples questioned him, saying, 'Why then do the scribes say that Elijah it behoveth to come first?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus answering said to them, 'Elijah doth indeed come first, and shall restore all things,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I say to you -- Elijah did already come, and they did not know him, but did with him whatever they would, so also the Son of Man is about to suffer by them.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then understood the disciples that concerning John the Baptist he spake to them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And when they came unto the multitude, there came to him a man, kneeling down to him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and saying, 'Sir, deal kindly with my son, for he is lunatic, and doth suffer miserably, for often he doth fall into the fire, and often into the water,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I brought him near to thy disciples, and they were not able to heal him.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus answering said, 'O generation, unstedfast and perverse, till when shall I be with you? till when shall I bear you? bring him to me hither;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jesus rebuked him, and the demon went out of him, and the lad was healed from that hour.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then the disciples having come to Jesus by himself, said, 'Wherefore were we not able to cast him out?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus said to them, 'Through your want of faith; for verily I say to you, if ye may have faith as a grain of mustard, ye shall say to this mount, Remove hence to yonder place, and it shall remove, and nothing shall be impossible to you,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and this kind doth not go forth except in prayer and fasting.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And while they are living in Galilee, Jesus said to them, 'The Son of Man is about to be delivered up to the hands of men,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they shall kill him, and the third day he shall rise,' and they were exceeding sorry.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they having come to Capernaum, those receiving the didrachms came near to Peter, and said, 'Your teacher -- doth he not pay the didrachms?' He saith, 'Yes.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And when he came into the house, Jesus anticipated him, saying, 'What thinkest thou, Simon? the kings of the earth -- from whom do they receive custom or poll-tax? from their sons or from the strangers?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Peter saith to him, 'From the strangers.' Jesus said to him, 'Then are the sons free;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but, that we may not cause them to stumble, having gone to the sea, cast a hook, and the fish that hath come up first take thou up, and having opened its mouth, thou shalt find a stater, that having taken, give to them for me and thee.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: At that hour came the disciples near to Jesus, saying, 'Who, now, is greater in the reign of the heavens?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus having called near a child, did set him in the midst of them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and said, 'Verily I say to you, if ye may not be turned and become as the children, ye may not enter into the reign of the heavens;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: whoever then may humble himself as this child, he is the greater in the reign of the heavens.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And he who may receive one such child in my name, doth receive me,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and whoever may cause to stumble one of those little ones who are believing in me, it is better for him that a weighty millstone may be hanged upon his neck, and he may be sunk in the depth of the sea.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Woe to the world from the stumbling-blocks! for there is a necessity for the stumbling-blocks to come, but woe to that man through whom the stumbling-block doth come!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And if thy hand or thy foot doth cause thee to stumble, cut them off and cast from thee; it is good for thee to enter into the life lame or maimed, rather than having two hands or two feet, to be cast to the fire the age-during.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And if thine eye doth cause thee to stumble, pluck it out and cast from thee; it is good for thee one-eyed to enter into the life, rather than having two eyes to be cast to the gehenna of the fire.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Beware! -- ye may not despise one of these little ones, for I say to you, that their messengers in the heavens do always behold the face of my Father who is in the heavens,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for the Son of Man did come to save the lost.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'What think ye? if a man may have an hundred sheep, and there may go astray one of them, doth he not -- having left the ninety-nine, having gone on the mountains -- seek that which is gone astray?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if it may come to pass that he doth find it, verily I say to you, that he doth rejoice over it more than over the ninety-nine that have not gone astray;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: so it is not will in presence of your Father who is in the heavens, that one of these little ones may perish.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And if thy brother may sin against thee, go and show him his fault between thee and him alone, if he may hear thee, thou didst gain thy brother;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if he may not hear, take with thee yet one or two, that by the mouth of two witnesses or three every word may stand.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And if he may not hear them, say [it] to the assembly, and if also the assembly he may not hear, let him be to thee as the heathen man and the tax-gatherer.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Verily I say to you, Whatever things ye may bind upon the earth shall be having been bound in the heavens, and whatever things ye may loose on the earth shall be having been loosed in the heavens.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Again, I say to you, that, if two of you may agree on the earth concerning anything, whatever they may ask -- it shall be done to them from my Father who is in the heavens,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for where there are two or three gathered together -- to my name, there am I in the midst of them.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then Peter having come near to him, said, 'Sir, how often shall my brother sin against me, and I forgive him -- till seven times?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jesus saith to him, 'I do not say to thee till seven times, but till seventy times seven.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Because of this was the reign of the heavens likened to a man, a king, who did will to take reckoning with his servants,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he having begun to take account, there was brought near to him one debtor of a myriad of talents,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he having nothing to pay, his lord did command him to be sold, and his wife, and the children, and all, whatever he had, and payment to be made.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The servant then, having fallen down, was bowing to him, saying, Sir, have patience with me, and I will pay thee all;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the lord of that servant having been moved with compassion did release him, and the debt he forgave him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And, that servant having come forth, found one of his fellow-servants who was owing him an hundred denaries, and having laid hold, he took him by the throat, saying, Pay me that which thou owest.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: His fellow-servant then, having fallen down at his feet, was calling on him, saying, Have patience with me, and I will pay thee all;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he would not, but having gone away, he cast him into prison, till he might pay that which was owing.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And his fellow-servants having seen the things that were done, were grieved exceedingly, and having come, shewed fully to their lord all the things that were done;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then having called him, his lord saith to him, Evil servant! all that debt I did forgive thee, seeing thou didst call upon me,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: did it not behove also thee to have dealt kindly with thy fellow-servant, as I also dealt kindly with thee?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And having been wroth, his lord delivered him to the inquisitors, till he might pay all that was owing to him;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: so also my heavenly Father will do to you, if ye may not forgive each one his brother from your hearts their trespasses.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass, when Jesus finished these words, he removed from Galilee, and did come to the borders of Judea, beyond the Jordan,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and great multitudes followed him, and he healed them there.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the Pharisees came near to him, tempting him, and saying to him, 'Is it lawful for a man to put away his wife for every cause?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he answering said to them, 'Did ye not read, that He who made [them], from the beginning a male and a female made them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and said, For this cause shall a man leave father and mother, and cleave to his wife, and they shall be -- the two -- for one flesh?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: so that they are no more two, but one flesh; what therefore God did join together, let no man put asunder.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They say to him, 'Why then did Moses command to give a roll of divorce, and to put her away?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He saith to them -- 'Moses for your stiffness of heart did suffer you to put away your wives, but from the beginning it hath not been so.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And I say to you, that, whoever may put away his wife, if not for whoredom, and may marry another, doth commit adultery; and he who did marry her that hath been put away, doth commit adultery.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: His disciples say to him, 'If the case of the man with the woman is so, it is not good to marry.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he said to them, 'All do not receive this word, but those to whom it hath been given;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for there are eunuchs who from the mother's womb were so born; and there are eunuchs who were made eunuchs by men; and there are eunuchs who kept themselves eunuchs because of the reign of the heavens: he who is able to receive [it] -- let him receive.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then were brought near to him children that he might put hands on them and pray, and the disciples rebuked them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: But Jesus said, 'Suffer the children, and forbid them not, to come unto me, for of such is the reign of the heavens;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having laid on them [his] hands, he departed thence.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And lo, one having come near, said to him, 'Good teacher, what good thing shall I do, that I may have life age-during?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he said to him, 'Why me dost thou call good? no one [is] good except One -- God; but if thou dost will to enter into the life, keep the commands.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He saith to him, 'What kind?' And Jesus said, 'Thou shalt not kill, thou shalt not commit adultery, thou shalt not steal, thou shalt not bear false witness,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: honour thy father and mother, and, thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The young man saith to him, 'All these did I keep from my youth; what yet do I lack?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jesus said to him, 'If thou dost will to be perfect, go away, sell what thou hast, and give to the poor, and thou shalt have treasure in heaven, and come, follow me.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the young man, having heard the word, went away sorrowful, for he had many possessions;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jesus said to his disciples, 'Verily I say to you, that hardly shall a rich man enter into the reign of the heavens;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and again I say to you, it is easier for a camel through the eye of a needle to go, than for a rich man to enter into the reign of God.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And his disciples having heard, were amazed exceedingly, saying, 'Who, then, is able to be saved?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus having earnestly beheld, said to them, 'With men this is impossible, but with God all things are possible.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then Peter answering said to him, 'Lo, we did leave all, and follow thee, what then shall we have?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus said to them, 'Verily I say to you, that ye who did follow me, in the regeneration, when the Son of Man may sit upon a throne of his glory, shall sit -- ye also -- upon twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and every one who left houses, or brothers, or sisters, or father, or mother, or wife, or children, or fields, for my name's sake, an hundredfold shall receive, and life age-during shall inherit;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and many first shall be last, and last first.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'For the reign of the heavens is like to a man, a householder, who went forth with the morning to hire workmen for his vineyard,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having agreed with the workmen for a denary a day, he sent them into his vineyard.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And having gone forth about the third hour, he saw others standing in the market-place idle,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and to these he said, Go ye -- also ye -- to the vineyard, and whatever may be righteous I will give you;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they went away. 'Again, having gone forth about the sixth and the ninth hour, he did in like manner.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And about the eleventh hour, having gone forth, he found others standing idle, and saith to them, Why here have ye stood all the day idle?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: they say to him, Because no one did hire us; he saith to them, Go ye -- ye also -- to the vineyard, and whatever may be righteous ye shall receive.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And evening having come, the lord of the vineyard saith to his steward, Call the workmen, and pay them the reward, having begun from the last -- unto the first.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they of about the eleventh hour having come, did receive each a denary.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And the first having come, did suppose that they shall receive more, and they received, they also, each a denary,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having received [it], they were murmuring against the householder, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: that These, the last, wrought one hour, and thou didst make them equal to us, who were bearing the burden of the day -- and the heat.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And he answering said to one of them, Comrade, I do no unrighteousness to thee; for a denary didst not thou agree with me?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: take that which is thine, and go; and I will to give to this, the last, also as to thee;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: is it not lawful to me to do what I will in mine own? is thine eye evil because I am good?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: So the last shall be first, and the first last, for many are called, and few chosen.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus going up to Jerusalem, took the twelve disciples by themselves in the way, and said to them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Lo, we go up to Jerusalem, and the Son of Man shall be delivered to the chief priests and scribes,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they shall condemn him to death, and shall deliver him to the nations to mock, and to scourge, and to crucify, and the third day he will rise again.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then came near to him the mother of the sons of Zebedee, with her sons, bowing and asking something from him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he said to her, 'What wilt thou?' She saith to him, 'Say, that they may sit -- these my two sons -- one on thy right hand, and one on the left, in thy reign.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus answering said, 'Ye have not known what ye ask for yourselves; are ye able to drink of the cup that I am about to drink? and with the baptism that I am baptized with, to be baptized?' They say to him, 'We are able.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he saith to them, 'Of my cup indeed ye shall drink, and with the baptism that I am baptized with ye shall be baptized; but to sit on my right hand and on my left is not mine to give, but -- to those for whom it hath been prepared by my father.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the ten having heard, were much displeased with the two brothers,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jesus having called them near, said, 'Ye have known that the rulers of the nations do exercise lordship over them, and those great do exercise authority over them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but not so shall it be among you, but whoever may will among you to become great, let him be your ministrant;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and whoever may will among you to be first, let him be your servant;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: even as the Son of Man did not come to be ministered to, but to minister, and to give his life a ransom for many.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they going forth from Jericho, there followed him a great multitude,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and lo, two blind men sitting by the way, having heard that Jesus doth pass by, cried, saying, 'Deal kindly with us, sir -- Son of David.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the multitude charged them that they might be silent, and they cried out the more, saying, 'Deal kindly with us sir -- Son of David.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having stood, Jesus called them, and said, 'What will ye [that] I may do to you?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: they say to him, 'Sir, that our eyes may be opened;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having been moved with compassion, Jesus touched their eyes, and immediately their eyes received sight, and they followed him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And when they came nigh to Jerusalem, and came to Bethphage, unto the mount of the Olives, then Jesus sent two disciples,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: saying to them, 'Go on to the village over-against you, and immediately ye shall find an ass bound, and a colt with her -- having loosed, bring ye to me;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if any one may say anything to you, ye shall say, that the lord hath need of them, and immediately he will send them.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And all this came to pass, that it might be fulfilled that was spoken through the prophet, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Tell ye the daughter of Zion, Lo, thy king doth come to thee, meek, and mounted on an ass, and a colt, a foal of a beast of burden.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the disciples having gone and having done as Jesus commanded them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: brought the ass and the colt, and did put on them their garments, and set [him] upon them;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the very great multitude spread their own garments in the way, and others were cutting branches from the trees, and were strewing in the way,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the multitudes who were going before, and who were following, were crying, saying, 'Hosanna to the Son of David, blessed is he who is coming in the name of the Lord; Hosanna in the highest.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he having entered into Jerusalem, all the city was moved, saying, 'Who is this?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the multitudes said, 'This is Jesus the prophet, who [is] from Nazareth of Galilee.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus entered into the temple of God, and did cast forth all those selling and buying in the temple, and the tables of the money-changers he overturned, and the seats of those selling the doves,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he saith to them, 'It hath been written, My house a house of prayer shall be called, but ye did make it a den of robbers.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And there came to him blind and lame men in the temple, and he healed them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the chief priests and the scribes having seen the wonderful things that he did, and the children crying in the temple, and saying, 'Hosanna to the Son of David,' were much displeased;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they said to him, 'Hearest thou what these say?' And Jesus saith to them, 'Yes, did ye never read, that, Out of the mouth of babes and sucklings Thou didst prepare praise?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having left them, he went forth out of the city to Bethany, and did lodge there,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and in the morning turning back to the city, he hungered,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having seen a certain fig-tree on the way, he came to it, and found nothing in it except leaves only, and he saith to it, 'No more from thee may fruit be -- to the age;' and forthwith the fig-tree withered.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the disciples having seen, did wonder, saying, 'How did the fig-tree forthwith wither?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus answering said to them, 'Verily I say to you, If ye may have faith, and may not doubt, not only this of the fig-tree shall ye do, but even if to this mount ye may say, Be lifted up and be cast into the sea, it shall come to pass;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and all -- as much as ye may ask in the prayer, believing, ye shall receive.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he having come to the temple, there came to him when teaching the chief priests and the elders of the people, saying, 'By what authority dost thou do these things? and who gave thee this authority?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus answering said to them, 'I will ask you -- I also -- one word, which if ye may tell me, I also will tell you by what authority I do these things;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the baptism of John, whence was it? -- from heaven, or from men?' And they were reasoning with themselves, saying, 'If we should say, From heaven; he will say to us, Wherefore, then, did ye not believe him?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if we should say, From men, we fear the multitude, for all hold John as a prophet.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And answering Jesus they said, 'We have not known.' He said to them -- he also -- 'Neither do I tell you by what authority I do these things.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And what think ye? A man had two children, and having come to the first, he said, Child, go, to-day be working in my vineyard.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he answering said, 'I will not,' but at last, having repented, he went.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And having come to the second, he said in the same manner, and he answering said, I [go], sir, and went not;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: which of the two did the will of the father?' They say to him, 'The first.' Jesus saith to them, 'Verily I say to you, that the tax-gatherers and the harlots do go before you into the reign of God,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for John came unto you in the way of righteousness, and ye did not believe him, and the tax-gatherers and the harlots did believe him, and ye, having seen, repented not at last -- to believe him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Hear ye another simile: There was a certain man, a householder, who planted a vineyard, and did put a hedge round it, and digged in it a wine-press, and built a tower, and gave it out to husbandmen, and went abroad.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And when the season of the fruits came nigh, he sent his servants unto the husbandmen, to receive the fruits of it,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the husbandmen having taken his servants, one they scourged, and one they killed, and one they stoned.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Again he sent other servants more than the first, and they did to them in the same manner.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And at last he sent unto them his son, saying, They will reverence my son;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the husbandmen having seen the son, said among themselves, This is the heir, come, we may kill him, and may possess his inheritance;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having taken him, they cast [him] out of the vineyard, and killed him;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: whenever therefore the lord of the vineyard may come, what will he do to these husbandmen?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They say to him, 'Evil men -- he will evilly destroy them, and the vineyard will give out to other husbandmen, who will give back to him the fruits in their seasons.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jesus saith to them, 'Did ye never read in the Writings, A stone that the builders disallowed, it became head of a corner; from the Lord hath this come to pass, and it is wonderful in our eyes.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Because of this I say to you, that the reign of God shall be taken from you, and given to a nation bringing forth its fruit;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he who is falling on this stone shall be broken, and on whomsoever it may fall it will crush him to pieces.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the chief priests and the Pharisees having heard his similes, knew that of them he speaketh,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and seeking to lay hold on him, they feared the multitudes, seeing they were holding him as a prophet.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus answering, again spake to them in similes, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'The reign of the heavens was likened to a man, a king, who made marriage-feasts for his son,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he sent forth his servants to call those having been called to the marriage-feasts, and they were not willing to come.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Again he sent forth other servants, saying, Say to those who have been called: Lo, my dinner I prepared, my oxen and the fatlings have been killed, and all things [are] ready, come ye to the marriage-feasts;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they, having disregarded [it], went away, the one to his own field, and the other to his merchandise;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the rest, having laid hold on his servants, did insult and slay [them].

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And the king having heard, was wroth, and having sent forth his soldiers, he destroyed those murderers, and their city he set on fire;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then saith he to his servants, The marriage-feast indeed is ready, and those called were not worthy,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: be going, then, on to the cross-ways, and as many as ye may find, call ye to the marriage-feasts.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And those servants, having gone forth to the ways, did gather all, as many as they found, both bad and good, and the marriage-feast apartment was filled with those reclining.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And the king having come in to view those reclining, saw there a man not clothed with clothing of the marriage-feast,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he saith to him, Comrade, how didst thou come in hither, not having clothing of the marriage-feast? and he was speechless.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Then said the king to the ministrants, Having bound his feet and hands, take him up and cast forth to the outer darkness, there shall be the weeping and the gnashing of the teeth;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for many are called, and few chosen.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then the Pharisees having gone, took counsel how they might ensnare him in words,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they send to him their disciples with the Herodians, saying, 'Teacher, we have known that thou art true, and the way of God in truth thou dost teach, and thou art not caring for any one, for thou dost not look to the face of men;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: tell us, therefore, what dost thou think? is it lawful to give tribute to Caesar or not?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus having known their wickedness, said, 'Why me do ye tempt, hypocrites?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: show me the tribute-coin?' and they brought to him a denary;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he saith to them, 'Whose [is] this image and the inscription?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: they say to him, 'Caesar's;' then saith he to them, 'Render therefore the things of Caesar to Caesar, and the things of God to God;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having heard they wondered, and having left him they went away.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In that day there came near to him Sadducees, who are saying there is not a rising again, and they questioned him, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Teacher, Moses said, If any one may die not having children, his brother shall marry his wife, and shall raise up seed to his brother.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And there were with us seven brothers, and the first having married did die, and not having seed, he left his wife to his brother;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: in like manner also the second, and the third, unto the seventh,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and last of all died also the woman;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: therefore in the rising again, of which of the seven shall she be wife -- for all had her?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus answering said to them, 'Ye go astray, not knowing the Writings, nor the power of God;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for in the rising again they do not marry, nor are they given in marriage, but are as messengers of God in heaven.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And concerning the rising again of the dead, did ye not read that which was spoken to you by God, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob? God is not a God of dead men, but of living.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having heard, the multitudes were astonished at his teaching;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the Pharisees, having heard that he did silence the Sadducees, were gathered together unto him;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and one of them, a lawyer, did question, tempting him, and saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Teacher, which [is] the great command in the Law?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus said to him, 'Thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thine understanding --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: this is a first and great command;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the second [is] like to it, Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: on these -- the two commands -- all the law and the prophets do hang.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the Pharisees having been gathered together, Jesus did question them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: saying, 'What do ye think concerning the Christ? of whom is he son?' They say to him, 'Of David.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He saith to them, 'How then doth David in the Spirit call him lord, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The Lord said to my lord, Sit at my right hand, till I may make thine enemies thy footstool?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If then David doth call him lord, how is he his son?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And no one was able to answer him a word, nor durst any from that day question him any more.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then Jesus spake to the multitudes, and to his disciples,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: saying, 'On the seat of Moses sat down the scribes and the Pharisees;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: all, then, as much as they may say to you to observe, observe and do, but according to their works do not, for they say, and do not;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for they bind together burdens heavy and grievous to be borne, and lay upon the shoulders of men, but with their finger they will not move them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And all their works they do to be seen by men, and they make broad their phylacteries, and enlarge the fringes of their garments,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: they love also the chief couches in the supper, and the chief seats in the synagogues,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the salutations in the market-places, and to be called by men, Rabbi, Rabbi.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And ye -- ye may not be called Rabbi, for one is your director -- the Christ, and all ye are brethren;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye may not call [any] your father on the earth, for one is your Father, who is in the heavens,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: nor may ye be called directors, for one is your director -- the Christ.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the greater of you shall be your ministrant,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and whoever shall exalt himself shall be humbled, and whoever shall humble himself shall be exalted.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Woe to you, Scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! because ye shut up the reign of the heavens before men, for ye do not go in, nor those going in do ye suffer to enter.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Woe to you, Scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! because ye eat up the houses of the widows, and for a pretence make long prayers, because of this ye shall receive more abundant judgment.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Woe to you, Scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! because ye go round the sea and the dry land to make one proselyte, and whenever it may happen -- ye make him a son of gehenna twofold more than yourselves.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Woe to you, blind guides, who are saying, Whoever may swear by the sanctuary, it is nothing, but whoever may swear by the gold of the sanctuary -- is debtor!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Fools and blind! for which [is] greater, the gold, or the sanctuary that is sanctifying the gold?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And, whoever may swear by the altar, it is nothing; but whoever may swear by the gift that is upon it -- is debtor!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Fools and blind! for which [is] greater, the gift, or the altar that is sanctifying the gift?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'He therefore who did swear by the altar, doth swear by it, and by all things on it;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he who did swear by the sanctuary, doth swear by it, and by Him who is dwelling in it;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he who did swear by the heaven, doth swear by the throne of God, and by Him who is sitting upon it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Woe to you, Scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! because ye give tithe of the mint, and the dill, and the cumin, and did neglect the weightier things of the Law -- the judgment, and the kindness, and the faith; these it behoved [you] to do, and those not to neglect.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Blind guides! who are straining out the gnat, and the camel are swallowing.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Woe to you, Scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! because ye make clean the outside of the cup and the plate, and within they are full of rapine and incontinence.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Blind Pharisee! cleanse first the inside of the cup and the plate, that the outside of them also may become clean.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Woe to you, Scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! because ye are like to whitewashed sepulchres, which outwardly indeed do appear beautiful, and within are full of bones of dead men, and of all uncleanness;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: so also ye outwardly indeed do appear to men righteous, and within ye are full of hypocrisy and lawlessness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Woe to you, Scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! because ye build the sepulchres of the prophets, and adorn the tombs of the righteous,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and say, If we had been in the days of our fathers, we would not have been partakers with them in the blood of the prophets.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: So that ye testify to yourselves, that ye are sons of them who did murder the prophets;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye -- ye fill up the measure of your fathers.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Serpents! brood of vipers! how may ye escape from the judgment of the gehenna?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Because of this, lo, I send to you prophets, and wise men, and scribes, and of them ye will kill and crucify, and of them ye will scourge in your synagogues, and will pursue from city to city;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: that on you may come all the righteous blood being poured out on the earth from the blood of Abel the righteous, unto the blood of Zacharias son of Barachias, whom ye slew between the sanctuary and the altar:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: verily I say to you, all these things shall come upon this generation.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Jerusalem, Jerusalem, that art killing the prophets, and stoning those sent unto thee, how often did I will to gather thy children together, as a hen doth gather her own chickens under the wings, and ye did not will.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, left desolate to you is your house;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for I say to you, ye may not see me henceforth, till ye may say, Blessed [is] he who is coming in the name of the Lord.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having gone forth, Jesus departed from the temple, and his disciples came near to show him the buildings of the temple,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jesus said to them, 'Do ye not see all these? verily I say to you, There may not be left here a stone upon a stone, that shall not be thrown down.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And when he is sitting on the mount of the Olives, the disciples came near to him by himself, saying, 'Tell us, when shall these be? and what [is] the sign of thy presence, and of the full end of the age?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus answering said to them, 'Take heed that no one may lead you astray,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for many shall come in my name, saying, I am the Christ, and they shall lead many astray,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and ye shall begin to hear of wars, and reports of wars; see, be not troubled, for it behoveth all [these] to come to pass, but the end is not yet.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'For nation shall rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom, and there shall be famines, and pestilences, and earthquakes, in divers places;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and all these [are] the beginning of sorrows;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then they shall deliver you up to tribulation, and shall kill you, and ye shall be hated by all the nations because of my name;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and then shall many be stumbled, and they shall deliver up one another, and shall hate one another.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And many false prophets shall arise, and shall lead many astray;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and because of the abounding of the lawlessness, the love of the many shall become cold;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but he who did endure to the end, he shall be saved;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and this good news of the reign shall be proclaimed in all the world, for a testimony to all the nations; and then shall the end arrive.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Whenever, therefore, ye may see the abomination of the desolation, that was spoken of through Daniel the prophet, standing in the holy place (whoever is reading let him observe)

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then those in Judea -- let them flee to the mounts;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: he on the house-top -- let him not come down to take up any thing out of his house;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he in the field -- let him not turn back to take his garments.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And woe to those with child, and to those giving suck in those days;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and pray ye that your flight may not be in winter, nor on a sabbath;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for there shall be then great tribulation, such as was not from the beginning of the world till now, no, nor may be.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And if those days were not shortened, no flesh would have been saved; but because of the chosen, shall those days be shortened.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Then if any one may say to you, Lo, here [is] the Christ! or here! ye may not believe;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for there shall arise false Christs, and false prophets, and they shall give great signs and wonders, so as to lead astray, if possible, also the chosen.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, I did tell you beforehand.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'If therefore they may say to you, Lo, in the wilderness he is, ye may not go forth; lo, in the inner chambers, ye may not believe;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for as the lightning doth come forth from the east, and doth appear unto the west, so shall be also the presence of the Son of Man;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for wherever the carcase may be, there shall the eagles be gathered together.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And immediately after the tribulation of those days, the sun shall be darkened, and the moon shall not give her light, and the stars shall fall from the heaven, and the powers of the heavens shall be shaken;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and then shall appear the sign of the Son of Man in the heaven; and then shall all the tribes of the earth smite the breast, and they shall see the Son of Man coming upon the clouds of the heaven, with power and much glory;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he shall send his messengers with a great sound of a trumpet, and they shall gather together his chosen from the four winds, from the ends of the heavens unto the ends thereof.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And from the fig-tree learn ye the simile: When already its branch may have become tender, and the leaves it may put forth, ye know that summer [is] nigh,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: so also ye, when ye may see all these, ye know that it is nigh -- at the doors.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Verily I say to you, this generation may not pass away till all these may come to pass.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The heaven and the earth shall pass away, but my words shall not pass away.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And concerning that day and the hour no one hath known -- not even the messengers of the heavens -- except my Father only;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and as the days of Noah -- so shall be also the presence of the Son of Man;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for as they were, in the days before the flood, eating, and drinking, marrying, and giving in marriage, till the day Noah entered into the ark,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they did not know till the flood came and took all away; so shall be also the presence of the Son of Man.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then two men shall be in the field, the one is received, and the one is left;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: two women shall be grinding in the mill, one is received, and one is left.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Watch ye therefore, because ye have not known in what hour your Lord doth come;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and this know, that if the master of the house had known in what watch the thief doth come, he had watched, and not suffered his house to be broken through;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: because of this also ye, become ye ready, because in what hour ye do not think, the Son of Man doth come.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Who, then, is the servant, faithful and wise, whom his lord did set over his household, to give them the nourishment in season?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Happy that servant, whom his lord, having come, shall find doing so;
