translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: verily I say to you, that over all his substance he will set him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And, if that evil servant may say in his heart, My Lord doth delay to come,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and may begin to beat the fellow-servants, and to eat and to drink with the drunken,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the lord of that servant will arrive in a day when he doth not expect, and in an hour of which he doth not know,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and will cut him off, and his portion with the hypocrites will appoint; there shall be the weeping and the gnashing of the teeth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Then shall the reign of the heavens be likened to ten virgins, who, having taken their lamps, went forth to meet the bridegroom;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and five of them were prudent, and five foolish;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: they who were foolish having taken their lamps, did not take with themselves oil;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the prudent took oil in their vessels, with their lamps.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And the bridegroom tarrying, they all nodded and were sleeping,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and in the middle of the night a cry was made, Lo, the bridegroom doth come; go ye forth to meet him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Then rose all those virgins, and trimmed their lamps,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the foolish said to the prudent, Give us of your oil, because our lamps are going out;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the prudent answered, saying -- Lest there may not be sufficient for us and you, go ye rather unto those selling, and buy for yourselves.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And while they are going away to buy, the bridegroom came, and those ready went in with him to the marriage-feasts, and the door was shut;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and afterwards come also do the rest of the virgins, saying, Sir, sir, open to us;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he answering said, Verily I say to you, I have not known you.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Watch therefore, for ye have not known the day nor the hour in which the Son of Man doth come.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'For -- as a man going abroad did call his own servants, and did deliver to them his substance,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and to one he gave five talents, and to another two, and to another one, to each according to his several ability, went abroad immediately.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And he who did receive the five talents, having gone, wrought with them, and made other five talents;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: in like manner also he who [received] the two, he gained, also he, other two;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he who did receive the one, having gone away, digged in the earth, and hid his lord's money.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And after a long time cometh the lord of those servants, and taketh reckoning with them;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he who did receive the five talents having come, brought other five talents, saying, 'Sir, five talents thou didst deliver to me; lo, other five talents did I gain besides them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And his lord said to him, Well done, servant, good and faithful, over a few things thou wast faithful, over many things I will set thee; enter into the joy of thy lord.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And he who also did receive the two talents having come, said, Sir, two talents thou didst deliver to me; lo, other two talents I did gain besides them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'His lord said to him, Well done, servant, good and faithful, over a few things thou wast faithful, over many things I will set thee; enter into the joy of thy lord.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And he also who hath received the one talent having come, said, Sir, I knew thee, that thou art a hard man, reaping where thou didst not sow, and gathering from whence thou didst not scatter;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having been afraid, having gone away, I hid thy talent in the earth; lo, thou hast thine own!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And his lord answering said to him, Evil servant, and slothful, thou hadst known that I reap where I did not sow, and I gather whence I did not scatter!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: it behoved thee then to put my money to the money-lenders, and having come I had received mine own with increase.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Take therefore from him the talent, and give to him having the ten talents,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for to every one having shall be given, and he shall have overabundance, and from him who is not having, even that which he hath shall be taken from him;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the unprofitable servant cast ye forth to the outer darkness; there shall be the weeping and the gnashing of the teeth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And whenever the Son of Man may come in his glory, and all the holy messengers with him, then he shall sit upon a throne of his glory;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and gathered together before him shall be all the nations, and he shall separate them from one another, as the shepherd doth separate the sheep from the goats,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he shall set the sheep indeed on his right hand, and the goats on the left.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Then shall the king say to those on his right hand, Come ye, the blessed of my Father, inherit the reign that hath been prepared for you from the foundation of the world;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for I did hunger, and ye gave me to eat; I did thirst, and ye gave me to drink; I was a stranger, and ye received me;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: naked, and ye put around me; I was infirm, and ye looked after me; in prison I was, and ye came unto me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Then shall the righteous answer him, saying, Lord, when did we see thee hungering, and we nourished? or thirsting, and we gave to drink?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and when did we see thee a stranger, and we received? or naked, and we put around?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and when did we see thee infirm, or in prison, and we came unto thee?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And the king answering, shall say to them, Verily I say to you, Inasmuch as ye did [it] to one of these my brethren -- the least -- to me ye did [it].

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then shall he say also to those on the left hand, Go ye from me, the cursed, to the fire, the age-during, that hath been prepared for the Devil and his messengers;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for I did hunger, and ye gave me not to eat; I did thirst, and ye gave me not to drink;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: a stranger I was, and ye did not receive me; naked, and ye put not around me; infirm, and in prison, and ye did not look after me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Then shall they answer, they also, saying, Lord, when did we see thee hungering, or thirsting, or a stranger, or naked, or infirm, or in prison, and we did not minister to thee?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Then shall he answer them, saying, Verily I say to you, Inasmuch as ye did [it] not to one of these, the least, ye did [it] not to me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And these shall go away to punishment age-during, but the righteous to life age-during.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it came to pass, when Jesus finished all these words, he said to his disciples,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Ye have known that after two days the passover cometh, and the Son of Man is delivered up to be crucified.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then were gathered together the chief priests, and the scribes, and the elders of the people, to the court of the chief priest who was called Caiaphas;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they consulted together that they might take Jesus by guile, and kill [him],

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they said, 'Not in the feast, that there may not be a tumult among the people.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus having been in Bethany, in the house of Simon the leper,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: there came to him a woman having an alabaster box of ointment, very precious, and she poured on his head as he is reclining (at meat).

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having seen [it], his disciples were much displeased, saying, 'To what purpose [is] this waste?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for this ointment could have been sold for much, and given to the poor.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus having known, said to them, 'Why do ye give trouble to the woman? for a good work she wrought for me;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for the poor always ye have with you, and me ye have not always;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for she having put this ointment on my body -- for my burial she did [it].

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Verily I say to you, Wherever this good news may be proclaimed in the whole world, what this [one] did shall also be spoken of -- for a memorial of her.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then one of the twelve, who is called Judas Iscariot, having gone unto the chief priests, said,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'What are ye willing to give me, and I will deliver him up to you?' and they weighed out to him thirty silverlings,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and from that time he was seeking a convenient season to deliver him up.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And on the first [day] of the unleavened food came the disciples near to Jesus, saying to him, 'Where wilt thou [that] we may prepare for thee to eat the passover?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he said, 'Go away to the city, unto such a one, and say to him, The Teacher saith, My time is nigh; near thee I keep the passover, with my disciples;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the disciples did as Jesus appointed them, and prepared the passover.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And evening having come, he was reclining (at meat) with the twelve,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and while they are eating, he said, 'Verily I say to you, that one of you shall deliver me up.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And being grieved exceedingly, they began to say to him, each of them, 'Is it I, Sir?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he answering said, 'He who did dip with me the hand in the dish, he will deliver me up;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: the Son of Man doth indeed go, as it hath been written concerning him, but woe to that man through whom the Son of Man is delivered up! good it were for him if that man had not been born.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Judas -- he who delivered him up -- answering said, 'Is it I, Rabbi?' He saith to him, 'Thou hast said.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And while they were eating, Jesus having taken the bread, and having blessed, did brake, and was giving to the disciples, and said, 'Take, eat, this is my body;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having taken the cup, and having given thanks, he gave to them, saying, 'Drink ye of it -- all;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for this is my blood of the new covenant, that for many is being poured out -- to remission of sins;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and I say to you, that I may not drink henceforth on this produce of the vine, till that day when I may drink it with you new in the reign of my Father.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having sung a hymn, they went forth to the mount of the Olives;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then saith Jesus to them, 'All ye shall be stumbled at me this night; for it hath been written, I will smite the shepherd, and the sheep of the flock shall be scattered abroad;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but, after my having risen, I will go before you to Galilee.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Peter answering said to him, 'Even if all shall be stumbled at thee, I will never be stumbled.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jesus said to him, 'Verily I say to thee, that, this night, before cock-crowing, thrice thou wilt deny me.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Peter saith to him, 'Even if it may be necessary for me to die with thee, I will not deny thee;' in like manner also said all the disciples.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then come with them doth Jesus to a place called Gethsemane, and he saith to the disciples, 'Sit ye here, till having gone away, I shall pray yonder.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having taken Peter, and the two sons of Zebedee, he began to be sorrowful, and to be very heavy;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then saith he to them, 'Exceedingly sorrowful is my soul -- unto death; abide ye here, and watch with me.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having gone forward a little, he fell on his face, praying, and saying, 'My Father, if it be possible, let this cup pass from me; nevertheless, not as I will, but as Thou.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he cometh unto the disciples, and findeth them sleeping, and he saith to Peter, 'So! ye were not able one hour to watch with me!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: watch, and pray, that ye may not enter into temptation: the spirit indeed is forward, but the flesh weak.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Again, a second time, having gone away, he prayed, saying, 'My Father, if this cup cannot pass away from me except I drink it, Thy will be done;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having come, he findeth them again sleeping, for their eyes were heavy.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having left them, having gone away again, he prayed a third time, saying the same word;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then cometh he unto his disciples, and saith to them, 'Sleep on henceforth, and rest! lo, the hour hath come nigh, and the Son of Man is delivered up to the hands of sinners.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Rise, let us go; lo, he hath come nigh who is delivering me up.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And while he is yet speaking, lo, Judas, one of the twelve did come, and with him a great multitude, with swords and sticks, from the chief priests and elders of the people.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he who did deliver him up did give them a sign, saying, 'Whomsoever I will kiss, it is he: lay hold on him;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and immediately, having come to Jesus, he said, 'Hail, Rabbi,' and kissed him;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jesus said to him, 'Comrade, for what art thou present?' Then having come near, they laid hands on Jesus, and took hold on him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And lo, one of those with Jesus, having stretched forth the hand, drew his sword, and having struck the servant of the chief priest, he took off his ear.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then saith Jesus to him, 'Turn back thy sword to its place; for all who did take the sword, by the sword shall perish;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: dost thou think that I am not able now to call upon my Father, and He will place beside me more than twelve legions of messengers?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: how then may the Writings be fulfilled, that thus it behoveth to happen?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In that hour said Jesus to the multitudes, 'As against a robber ye did come forth, with swords and sticks, to take me! daily with you I was sitting teaching in the temple, and ye did not lay hold on me;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but all this hath come to pass, that the Writings of the prophets may be fulfilled;' then all the disciples, having left him, fled.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And those laying hold on Jesus led [him] away unto Caiaphas the chief priest, where the scribes and the elders were gathered together,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Peter was following him afar off, unto the court of the chief priest, and having gone in within, he was sitting with the officers, to see the end.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the chief priests, and the elders, and all the council, were seeking false witness against Jesus, that they might put him to death,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they did not find; and many false witnesses having come near, they did not find; and at last two false witnesses having come near,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: said, 'This one said, I am able to throw down the sanctuary of God, and after three days to build it.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the chief priest having stood up, said to him, 'Nothing thou dost answer! what do these witness against thee?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Jesus was silent. And the chief priest answering said to him, 'I adjure thee, by the living God, that thou mayest say to us, if thou art the Christ -- the Son of God.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jesus saith to him, 'Thou hast said; nevertheless I say to you, hereafter ye shall see the Son of Man sitting on the right hand of the power, and coming upon the clouds, of the heaven.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then the chief priest rent his garments, saying, -- 'He hath spoken evil; what need have we yet of witnesses? lo, now ye heard his evil speaking;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: what think ye?' and they answering said, 'He is worthy of death.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then did they spit in his face and buffet him, and others did slap,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: saying, 'Declare to us, O Christ, who he is that struck thee?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Peter without was sitting in the court, and there came near to him a certain maid, saying, 'And thou wast with Jesus of Galilee!'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he denied before all, saying, 'I have not known what thou sayest.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he having gone forth to the porch, another female saw him, and saith to those there, 'And this one was with Jesus of Nazareth;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and again did he deny with an oath -- 'I have not known the man.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And after a little those standing near having come, said to Peter, 'Truly thou also art of them, for even thy speech doth make thee manifest.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then began he to anathematise, and to swear -- 'I have not known the man;' and immediately did a cock crow,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Peter remembered the saying of Jesus, he having said to him -- 'Before cock-crowing, thrice thou wilt deny me;' and having gone without, he did weep bitterly.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And morning having come, all the chief priests and the elders of the people took counsel against Jesus, so as to put him to death;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having bound him, they did lead away, and delivered him up to Pontius Pilate, the governor.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then Judas -- he who delivered him up -- having seen that he was condemned, having repented, brought back the thirty silverlings to the chief priests, and to the elders, saying,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'I did sin, having delivered up innocent blood;' and they said, 'What -- to us? thou shalt see!'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having cast down the silverlings in the sanctuary, he departed, and having gone away, he did strangle himself.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the chief priests having taken the silverlings, said, 'It is not lawful to put them to the treasury, seeing it is the price of blood;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having taken counsel, they bought with them the field of the potter, for the burial of strangers;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: therefore was that field called, 'Field of blood,' unto this day.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then was fulfilled that spoken through Jeremiah the prophet, saying, 'And I took the thirty silverlings, the price of him who hath been priced, whom they of the sons of Israel did price,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and gave them for the field of the potter, as the Lord did appoint to me.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus stood before the governor, and the governor did question him, saying, 'Art thou the king of the Jews!' And Jesus said to him, 'Thou sayest.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And in his being accused by the chief priests and the elders, he did not answer any thing,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then saith Pilate to him, 'Dost thou not hear how many things they witness against thee?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he did not answer him, not even to one word, so that the governor did wonder greatly.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And at the feast the governor had been accustomed to release one to the multitude, a prisoner, whom they willed,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they had then a noted prisoner, called Barabbas,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: they therefore having been gathered together, Pilate said to them, 'Whom will ye I shall release to you? Barabbas or Jesus who is called Christ?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: for he had known that because of envy they had delivered him up.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And as he is sitting on the tribunal, his wife sent unto him, saying, 'Nothing -- to thee and to that righteous one, for many things did I suffer to-day in a dream because of him.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the chief priests and the elders did persuade the multitudes that they might ask for themselves Barabbas, and might destroy Jesus;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the governor answering said to them, 'Which of the two will ye [that] I shall release to you?' And they said, 'Barabbas.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Pilate saith to them, 'What then shall I do with Jesus who is called Christ?' They all say to him, 'Let be crucified!'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the governor said, 'Why, what evil did he?' and they were crying out the more, saying, 'Let be crucified.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Pilate having seen that it profiteth nothing, but rather a tumult is made, having taken water, he did wash the hands before the multitude, saying, 'I am innocent from the blood of this righteous one; ye -- ye shall see;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and all the people answering said, 'His blood [is] upon us, and upon our children!'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then did he release to them Barabbas, and having scourged Jesus, he delivered [him] up that he may be crucified;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: then the soldiers of the governor having taken Jesus to the Praetorium, did gather to him all the band;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having unclothed him, they put around him a crimson cloak,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having plaited him a crown out of thorns they put [it] on his head, and a reed in his right hand, and having kneeled before him, they were mocking him, saying, 'Hail, the king of the Jews.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having spit on him, they took the reed, and were smiting on his head;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and when they had mocked him, they took off from him the cloak, and put on him his own garments, and led him away to crucify [him].

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And coming forth, they found a man, a Cyrenian, by name Simon: him they impressed that he might bear his cross;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having come to a place called Golgotha, that is called Place of a Skull,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: they gave him to drink vinegar mixed with gall, and having tasted, he would not drink.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having crucified him, they divided his garments, casting a lot, that it might be fulfilled that was spoken by the prophet, 'They divided my garments to themselves, and over my vesture they cast a lot;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and sitting down, they were watching him there,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they put up over his head, his accusation written, 'This is Jesus, the king of the Jews.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then crucified with him are two robbers, one on the right hand, and one on the left,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and those passing by were speaking evil of him, wagging their heads,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and saying, 'Thou that art throwing down the sanctuary, and in three days building [it], save thyself; if Son thou art of God, come down from the cross.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And in like manner also the chief priests mocking, with the scribes and elders, said,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Others he saved; himself he is not able to save! If he be King of Israel, let him come down now from the cross, and we will believe him;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: he hath trusted on God, let Him now deliver him, if He wish him, because he said -- Son of God I am;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: with the same also the robbers, who were crucified with him, were reproaching him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And from the sixth hour darkness came over all the land unto the ninth hour,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and about the ninth hour Jesus cried out with a great voice, saying, 'Eli, Eli, lama sabachthani?' that is, 'My God, my God, why didst Thou forsake me?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And certain of those standing there having heard, said -- 'Elijah he doth call;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and immediately, one of them having run, and having taken a spunge, having filled [it] with vinegar, and having put [it] on a reed, was giving him to drink,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: but the rest said, 'Let alone, let us see if Elijah doth come -- about to save him.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jesus having again cried with a great voice, yielded the spirit;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and lo, the vail of the sanctuary was rent in two from top unto bottom, and the earth did quake, and the rocks were rent,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the tombs were opened, and many bodies of the saints who have fallen asleep, arose,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having come forth out of the tombs after his rising, they went into the holy city, and appeared to many.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the centurion, and those with him watching Jesus, having seen the earthquake, and the things that were done, were exceedingly afraid, saying, 'Truly this was God's Son.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And there were there many women beholding from afar, who did follow Jesus from Galilee, ministering to him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: among whom was Mary the Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James and of Joses, and the mother of the sons of Zebedee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And evening having come, there came a rich man, from Arimathea, named Joseph, who also himself was discipled to Jesus,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: he having gone near to Pilate, asked for himself the body of Jesus; then Pilate commanded the body to be given back.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having taken the body, Joseph wrapped it in clean linen,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and laid it in his new tomb, that he hewed in the rock, and having rolled a great stone to the door of the tomb, he went away;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and there were there Mary the Magdalene, and the other Mary, sitting over-against the sepulchre.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And on the morrow that is after the preparation, were gathered together the chief priests, and the Pharisees, unto Pilate,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: saying, 'Sir, we have remembered that that deceiver said while yet living, After three days I do rise;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: command, then, the sepulchre to be made secure till the third day, lest his disciples, having come by night, may steal him away, and may say to the people, He rose from the dead, and the last deceit shall be worse than the first.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Pilate said to them, 'Ye have a watch, go away, make secure -- as ye have known;'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they, having gone, did make the sepulchre secure, having sealed the stone, together with the watch.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And on the eve of the sabbaths, at the dawn, toward the first of the sabbaths, came Mary the Magdalene, and the other Mary, to see the sepulchre,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and lo, there came a great earthquake, for a messenger of the Lord, having come down out of heaven, having come, did roll away the stone from the door, and was sitting upon it,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and his countenance was as lightning, and his clothing white as snow,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and from the fear of him did the keepers shake, and they became as dead men.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the messenger answering said to the women, 'Fear not ye, for I have known that Jesus, who hath been crucified, ye seek;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: he is not here, for he rose, as he said; come, see the place where the Lord was lying;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having gone quickly, say ye to his disciples, that he rose from the dead; and lo, he doth go before you to Galilee, there ye shall see him; lo, I have told you.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having gone forth quickly from the tomb, with fear and great joy, they ran to tell to his disciples;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and as they were going to tell to his disciples, then lo, Jesus met them, saying, 'Hail!' and they having come near, laid hold of his feet, and did bow to him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then saith Jesus to them, 'Fear ye not, go away, tell to my brethren that they may go away to Galilee, and there they shall see me.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And while they are going on, lo, certain of the watch having come to the city, told to the chief priests all the things that happened,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having been gathered together with the elders, counsel also having taken, they gave much money to the soldiers,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: saying, 'Say ye, that his disciples having come by night, stole him -- we being asleep;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and if this be heard by the governor, we will persuade him, and you keep free from anxiety.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they, having received the money, did as they were taught, and this account was spread abroad among Jews till this day.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the eleven disciples went to Galilee, to the mount where Jesus appointed them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and having seen him, they bowed to him, but some did waver.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And having come near, Jesus spake to them, saying, 'Given to me was all authority in heaven and on earth;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: having gone, then, disciple all the nations, (baptizing them -- to the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: teaching them to observe all, whatever I did command you,) and lo, I am with you all the days -- till the full end of the age.'
